image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Megiddo: Thutmose III at Karnak

details.war: Thutmose III's first campaign in the Levant
details.gloss: Thutmose III defeated a Canaanite coalition and secured Egyptian dominance in the Levant. The campaign is one of the earliest battles described in substantial detail.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kadesh: Egypt Abou Simbel6

details.outcome: Indecisive; Egyptian tactical survival and Hittite strategic check
details.gloss: Ramesses II and Muwatalli II fought one of the largest chariot battles of the Bronze Age. Its propaganda, diplomacy, and later treaty made it as famous as its battlefield result.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Marathon: Scene of the Battle of Marathon

details.gloss: Athenian and Plataean hoplites defeated a Persian expeditionary force. The victory strengthened Athenian confidence and became a lasting symbol of Greek resistance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Thermopylae: Léonidas aux Thermopyles Jacques Louis David Musée du Louvre Peintures INV 3690 ; L 3711

details.gloss: A small Greek rearguard delayed Xerxes' army at a narrow pass. The defeat became famous for disciplined resistance and bought time for wider Greek defense.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Salamis: Battle of salamis
image.attribution: The Department of History, United States Military Academy

details.gloss: The Greek fleet lured the larger Persian navy into confined waters and broke its offensive power. Salamis shifted the invasion toward Persian retreat.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Gaugamela: Pietro da Cortona Battle of Alexander versus Darius Google Art Project

details.gloss: Alexander defeated Darius III in the decisive battle of the Persian campaign. The victory opened the imperial heartland to Macedonian control.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Tyre: Siege tryre
image.attribution: Frank Martini. Cartographer, Department of History, United States Military Academy

details.gloss: Alexander used siege works and naval pressure to take the island city of Tyre. The operation displayed his willingness to solve strategic problems with engineering.
details.casualties: 34,000-39,000 (including 13,000 civilians and 2,000 executed soldiers)

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cannae: The Death of Paulus Aemilius at the Battle of Cannae (Yale University Art Gallery scan)

details.gloss: Hannibal enveloped a much larger Roman army and destroyed it in a classic double envelopment. Cannae became a benchmark for tactical annihilation.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Zama: The Battle Between Scipio and Hannibal at Zama

details.gloss: Scipio Africanus defeated Hannibal and ended Carthage's chance to dominate the western Mediterranean. The result forced a harsh peace on Carthage.
details.casualties: About 1,500 Roman dead; about 20,000 Carthaginian dead and 20,000 captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Alesia: Alise Sainte Reine Contrevallation

details.gloss: Caesar trapped Vercingetorix with siege lines while holding off a relief army. Alesia effectively ended organized Gallic resistance to Roman conquest.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Carrhae: Battle of Carrhae (Caesarː A History Of The Art Of War Among The Romans Down To The End, vol. II, 1892)

details.gloss: Parthian horse archers and cataphracts destroyed Crassus' Roman army. The defeat exposed Roman vulnerability beyond the Euphrates and shook Roman politics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Actium: Medinaceli Actium reliefs 06

combatants: Forces of Mark Antony and Cleopatra
details.war: Final War of the Roman Republic

series.label: Final War of the Roman Republic

details.gloss: Octavian's fleet defeated Antony and Cleopatra near western Greece. The victory cleared the way for Octavian to become Augustus and found the Roman Empire.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Teutoburg Forest: Epitaph des Marcus Caelius

details.gloss: Arminius ambushed and destroyed three Roman legions in forested terrain. The disaster helped halt Rome's permanent expansion east of the Rhine.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Red Cliffs: Chibi

combatants: Sun Quan and Liu Bei coalition
details.war: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty

series.label: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty

details.gloss: A southern coalition stopped Cao Cao's northern army in a river battle remembered for fire ships and strategy. The result helped shape the Three Kingdoms balance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Adrianople: Battle of Adrianople 378 en
image.attribution: Bitva_u_Adrianopole.gif : Elias84 derivative work: Dipa_1965 ( talk )

details.gloss: Gothic forces defeated and killed Emperor Valens. Adrianople exposed deep Roman military pressures and marked a major shock to imperial authority.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Yarmouk: Hayton BNF886 9v

details.gloss: The Rashidun army defeated a Byzantine field force and secured the conquest of Syria. Yarmouk transformed the political and religious map of the eastern Mediterranean.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of al-Qadisiyyah: Depiction of the Battle of al Qādisiyyah from a manuscript of the Persian epic Shāh nāmeh. Source British Library (MS. I.O.Islamic 3265 (1614) f. 602r)

details.gloss: Arab Muslim forces defeated the Sasanian army in Mesopotamia. The battle opened the road toward Ctesiphon and accelerated the collapse of Sasanian power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tours: Steuben Bataille de Poitiers

details.gloss: Charles Martel's Frankish army stopped an Umayyad raiding force in western Gaul. Later memory made Tours a defining episode in early medieval Europe.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Talas: Battle of Talas

details.gloss: Abbasid forces defeated a Tang army in Central Asia. The battle signaled the limits of Tang influence westward and became linked to wider cultural exchange.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hastings: Bayeux Tapestry scene57 Harold death

details.gloss: William of Normandy defeated Harold II and ended Anglo-Saxon rule in England. The result reshaped English aristocracy, law, language, and landholding.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Manzikert: Battle of Manzikert (1071)

details.gloss: Seljuk forces captured Emperor Romanos IV and weakened Byzantine control in Anatolia. The defeat helped open the region to Turkish settlement and power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hattin: Saladin Guy

details.gloss: Saladin destroyed the main crusader field army near the Horns of Hattin. The victory led directly to the capture of Jerusalem and the Third Crusade.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mohi: Battle of Mohi 1241

details.gloss: Mongol commanders used mobility, feigned retreat, and coordinated attacks to break the Hungarian army. The battle devastated Hungary during the western Mongol campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ain Jalut: Campaign of the Battle of Ain Jalut 1260

details.gloss: The Mamluks defeated a Mongol force in Palestine and checked Mongol expansion into Egypt. Ain Jalut became a landmark in the defense of the Islamic Near East.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Yamen: Jingmen002
image.attribution: (Long night without wind) 长夜无风

details.gloss: The Yuan defeated the last Southern Song fleet, ending the dynasty's resistance. The battle completed Mongol rule over China under Kublai Khan.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Third Battle of Bach Dang: Battle of Bach Dang river

details.gloss: Tran Hung Dao's forces trapped a Yuan fleet among concealed stakes in the tidal river. The victory ended the third Mongol invasion of Dai Viet.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Grunwald: Jan Matejko, Bitwa pod Grunwaldem

details.gloss: A Polish-Lithuanian coalition defeated the Teutonic Order in one of medieval Europe's largest battles. The result weakened the order's military and political position.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Agincourt: Schlacht von Azincourt
image.attribution: Antoine Leduc, Sylvie Leluc et Olivier Renaudeau (dir.), D'Azincourt à Marignan. Chevaliers et bombardes, 1415-1515 , Paris, Gallimard / Musée de l'armée, 2015, p. 18-19, ISBN 978-2-07-014949-0

details.gloss: Henry V's army defeated French forces on muddy ground with disciplined archery and defensive positioning. Agincourt became central to English military memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Constantinople: Siege of Constantinople fresco, Moldovița monastery, Vatra Moldoviței, 2017

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Mehmed II captured Constantinople after a major artillery-backed siege. The city's fall ended the Byzantine Empire and made Istanbul the Ottoman capital.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Towton: Richard Caton Woodville's The Battle of Towton

details.gloss: Yorkist forces won a brutal battle that helped secure Edward IV's claim to the English throne. Towton is remembered as one of England's bloodiest medieval battles.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Tenochtitlan: Conquista de México por Cortés Tenochtitlan Painting

details.war: Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire

series.label: Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire

details.gloss: Spanish and Indigenous allied forces besieged and captured the Mexica capital. The fall of Tenochtitlan remade power in central Mexico and began New Spain.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Pavia: The Battle of Pavia, 1525 (by Rupert Heller) Nationalmuseum, Stockholm

details.gloss: Imperial forces defeated and captured Francis I of France. Pavia showed the power of firearms and infantry coordination in early modern European war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Battle of Panipat: The battle of Panipat and the death of Sultan Ibrāhīm, the last of the Lōdī Sultans of Delhi
image.attribution: Ẓahīr ud-Dīn Muḥammad Bābur (1483-1530) It contains 30 mostly full-page miniatures in fine Mughal style by at least two different artists.

details.gloss: Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi with field artillery, matchlocks, and mobile tactics. The battle established Mughal power in northern India and began a new imperial era.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mohacs: Battle of Mohacs 1526

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Suleiman's army shattered the Hungarian royal host and King Louis II died in the retreat. The defeat opened Hungary to Ottoman and Habsburg rivalry.

image.alt: Map or diagram associated with Battle of Nagashino: Battle of Nagashino Map Folding Screen 1575

details.gloss: Oda and Tokugawa forces defeated Takeda cavalry near Nagashino Castle. The battle is famous for defensive works and the effective use of arquebusiers.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Lepanto: Laureys a Castro The Battle of Lepanto

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: A Holy League fleet defeated Ottoman naval forces in a vast galley battle. Lepanto checked Ottoman naval momentum in the central Mediterranean.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Alcacer Quibir: Lagos46 kopie

details.gloss: Moroccan forces defeated Portugal's invasion and King Sebastian was killed. The disaster triggered a Portuguese succession crisis and altered Iberian politics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Sekigahara: Sekigaharascreen
image.attribution: User LordAmeth on en.wikipedia Collection of The Town of Sekigahara Archive of History and Cultural Anthropology

details.gloss: Tokugawa Ieyasu defeated a rival coalition in a decisive struggle for national control. Sekigahara cleared the path to the Tokugawa shogunate.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Osaka: Osakarakuzyou (cropped)

details.gloss: Tokugawa forces destroyed the Toyotomi stronghold after winter and summer campaigns. Osaka ended the last major challenge to Tokugawa rule.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Breitenfeld: 29a Gustave Adolphe à la bataille de Breitenfeld, 1632

details.gloss: Gustavus Adolphus defeated the Catholic League and transformed the war's momentum. Breitenfeld displayed flexible Swedish tactics and revived Protestant fortunes.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Rocroi: La Bataille de Rocroi

details.gloss: French forces broke the Spanish tercios and announced France's rising military power. Rocroi became a symbol of the transition away from Spanish battlefield dominance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Vienna: Anonym Entsatz Wien 1683

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: A relief army led by John III Sobieski broke the Ottoman siege of Vienna. The victory marked a major turning point in Habsburg-Ottoman conflict.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Blenheim: Copy after Jan van Huchtenburgh (Haarlem 1647 Amsterdam 1733) The Battle of Blenheim (or Höchstädt), 1704. RCIN 404899 Royal Collection

details.gloss: Marlborough and Eugene defeated Franco-Bavarian forces and saved Vienna from pressure. Blenheim ended the aura of French invincibility under Louis XIV.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Plassey: Lord Clive meeting with Mir Jafar after the Battle of Plassey

combatants: Nawab of Bengal and French allies
details.gloss: Company forces under Robert Clive defeated the Nawab of Bengal after elite defections. Plassey opened the way for British political power in Bengal.
details.casualties: British: 22 killed and 50 wounded; Nawab's army: over 500 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Yorktown: Surrender of Lord Cornwallis

details.gloss: Cornwallis surrendered after American and French forces trapped his army by land and sea. Yorktown ended major British land operations in North America.
details.casualties: Allies: about 400 killed or wounded; British and German forces: about 600 killed or wounded and about 7,600 surrendered

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Austerlitz: La bataille d'Austerlitz. 2 decembre 1805 (François Gérard)

details.location: Near Slavkov u Brna, Czech Republic
details.gloss: Napoleon defeated the Russian and Austrian emperors through deception and a central counterstroke. Austerlitz is often treated as his tactical masterpiece.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Trafalgar: The Battle of Trafalgar by William Clarkson Stanfield

details.gloss: Nelson's fleet destroyed Franco-Spanish naval power while he was mortally wounded. Trafalgar secured British command of the seas during the Napoleonic Wars.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Borodino: Battle of Borodino

details.gloss: Napoleon won a costly battle on the road to Moscow, but failed to destroy the Russian army. Borodino showed the attritional danger of the Russian campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Leipzig: MoshkovVI SrazhLeypcigomGRM

details.gloss: Coalition armies defeated Napoleon in the largest battle of the Napoleonic Wars. Leipzig forced the French retreat from Germany and weakened the empire decisively.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Waterloo: Battle of Waterloo 1815

details.gloss: Wellington and Blucher defeated Napoleon's final army after a tense campaign in Belgium. Waterloo ended the Hundred Days, sent Napoleon into exile, and closed the Napoleonic era.
details.casualties: 47,000+ (not including prisoners and missing)

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ayacucho: Batalla de Ayacucho by Martín Tovar y Tovar (1827 1902)

details.gloss: Patriot forces defeated the main Spanish royalist army in Peru. Ayacucho effectively ended Spanish military power on the South American mainland.

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Siege of Sevastopol: Panorama dentro

combatants: France, Britain, Ottoman Empire, and Sardinia
details.gloss: Allied armies besieged Russia's Black Sea naval base for nearly a year. The campaign became a defining episode of the Crimean War and modern siege warfare.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Solferino: Yvon Bataille de Solferino Compiegne

details.gloss: A vast and chaotic battle helped push Austria from Lombardy. The suffering witnessed at Solferino inspired Henry Dunant and the Red Cross movement.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Gettysburg: Thure de Thulstrup L. Prang and Co. Battle of Gettysburg Restoration by Adam Cuerden

details.gloss: Union forces stopped Lee's invasion of Pennsylvania after three days of fighting. Gettysburg became the Civil War's symbolic turning point in the eastern theater.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Vicksburg: Thure de Thulstrup Siege of Vicksburg Assault on Fort Hill

details.gloss: Grant's campaign and siege forced the surrender of Vicksburg. The victory gave the Union control of the Mississippi River and split the Confederacy in two.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Koniggratz: Schlacht bei koeniggraetz von georg bleibtreu

combatants: Austrian Empire and Kingdom of Saxony
details.gloss: Prussia defeated Austria in a decisive battle shaped by rail mobilization, staff planning, and modern rifles. The result shifted German leadership toward Prussia.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Isandlwana: Isandhlwana

details.gloss: Zulu forces overwhelmed a British camp in one of Britain's worst colonial defeats. The battle showed the danger of underestimating local armies and terrain.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Adwa: Adoua 1
image.attribution: The Graphic , from details supplied by survivors

details.gloss: Ethiopia defeated an Italian invasion and preserved its independence during the Scramble for Africa. Adwa became a major symbol of anti-colonial resistance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Omdurman: 21lancers
image.attribution: Edward Matthew Hale (1852-1924). Original uploader was Raoulduke47 4 January 2007 (original upload date) at en.wikipedia

details.gloss: Kitchener's army used rifles, artillery, and machine guns to defeat Mahdist forces. Omdurman illustrated the lethal imbalance of late imperial warfare.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Port Arthur: RusShellJapLine1905

details.gloss: Japanese forces captured Russia's fortified naval base after costly assaults and siege operations. Port Arthur signaled Japan's arrival as a major military power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mukden: Russian Field Gun during the Battle of Mukden

details.gloss: Japan defeated Russian armies in a huge land battle in Manchuria. Mukden helped force Russia toward peace and shocked observers of imperial power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tsushima: Battle of Tsushima, 1905 by Tōjō Shōtarō

details.gloss: Japan destroyed Russia's Baltic Fleet after its long voyage to East Asia. Tsushima was a decisive naval victory and a landmark in modern sea power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tannenberg: Russian prisoners tannenberg

details.gloss: German forces encircled and destroyed a Russian army in East Prussia. Tannenberg made Hindenburg and Ludendorff famous and stabilized Germany's eastern front.

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Battle of the Marne: German soldiers Battle of Marne WWI

details.gloss: French and British forces stopped the German advance toward Paris. The battle ended hopes for a quick German victory and led toward trench warfare.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Gallipoli Campaign: Anzac, the landing 1915

details.gloss: Ottoman defenders defeated an Allied attempt to force the Dardanelles and open a route to Russia. Gallipoli shaped Turkish, Australian, and New Zealand memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Verdun: French soldiers of the 87th Regiment shelter in their trenches at Hill 304 at Verdun

details.gloss: Verdun became a grinding battle of endurance between France and Germany. Its scale, duration, and slogan of resistance made it a defining symbol of World War I.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Somme: British Mark I male tank Somme 25 September 1916

details.outcome: Allied strategic pressure with limited territorial gains
details.gloss: The Somme combined mass infantry attacks, artillery, and early tank use in a costly campaign. It remains a central image of World War I attrition.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Passchendaele: Chateauwood

details.outcome: Allied tactical victory with severe losses
details.gloss: Fighting around Ypres sank into mud, artillery, and attrition. Passchendaele became shorthand for the suffering and disputed gains of trench warfare.
details.casualties: British-commanded armies: about 275,000 casualties; German casualties also heavy and disputed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Caporetto: Battle of Caporetto
image.attribution: History Department of the US Military Academy West Point

details.gloss: Austro-German forces broke the Italian line with infiltration tactics and gas. Caporetto caused a major Italian retreat and forced Allied support.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Megiddo: Battle of Megiddo (1918) Destroyed Turkish transport

details.gloss: Allenby's forces broke Ottoman defenses in Palestine through deception, cavalry exploitation, and air attacks. The victory led to the collapse of Ottoman control in Syria.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Amiens: 8th August 1918 (Will Longstaff)

details.gloss: Allied troops used surprise, tanks, aircraft, and coordinated infantry to break German lines. Amiens opened the Hundred Days Offensive.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Shanghai: Shanghai1937KMT machine gun nest

details.gloss: China committed major forces to a brutal urban battle to resist Japan and draw world attention. Shanghai showed the scale of the war before World War II widened.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Warsaw: The Royal Castle in Warsaw burning 17.09.1939

details.gloss: Warsaw held out under bombing and siege during Germany's invasion of Poland. Its fall marked the collapse of organized Polish defense in the capital.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dunkirk: Soldados británicos en Dunkerque en 1940

details.gloss: German forces trapped Allied armies near the coast, but a massive evacuation rescued much of the British Expeditionary Force. Dunkirk became both defeat and deliverance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Moscow: RIAN archive 887721 Defense of Moscow

details.gloss: Soviet defenses and counterattacks stopped Germany's drive on Moscow. The battle ended the expectation of a quick German victory in the east.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Singapore: Surrender Singapore
image.attribution: Photographer not identified. "Official photograph". Post-Work: User:W.wolny

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Japanese forces captured Britain's major Southeast Asian base after a rapid Malayan campaign. The fall of Singapore was a severe shock to British imperial prestige.
details.casualties: Malaya and Singapore campaign: 8,708 Commonwealth killed or wounded and 130,000 captured; 9,824 Japanese casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bataan: Philippine Islands Jap tanks full

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: American and Filipino defenders held Bataan for months before surrendering. The battle delayed Japan but led to the Bataan Death March.
details.casualties: 30,000 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Midway: USS Yorktown (CV 5) is hit by a torpedo on 4 June 1942

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: American codebreaking and carrier aviation destroyed four Japanese carriers. Midway shifted initiative in the Pacific War toward the United States.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Guadalcanal Campaign: Marines rest in the field on Guadalcanal
image.attribution: Marines rest in the field on Guadalcanal

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Allied forces fought on land, sea, and air to hold Guadalcanal. The campaign became the first major Allied offensive victory in the Pacific.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Stalingrad: Расчет советской 76,2 мм пушки ЗиС 3 ведет огонь в Сталинграде. Ноябрь 1942 г

details.gloss: Soviet forces encircled and destroyed the German Sixth Army after months of urban warfare. Stalingrad became the eastern front's decisive turning point.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Second Battle of El Alamein: El Alamein 1942 British infantry

details.gloss: Montgomery's army broke Axis positions in Egypt and forced a retreat across North Africa. El Alamein ended the Axis threat to the Suez Canal.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kursk: Soviet troops and T 34 tanks counterattacking Kursk Voronezh Front July 1943

details.gloss: Germany's offensive against the Kursk salient failed against layered Soviet defenses. The battle confirmed Soviet strategic initiative on the eastern front.

image.alt: Photograph of Monte Cassino monastery ruins after bombing in 1944.

details.gloss: Allied armies fought through the Gustav Line around Monte Cassino after repeated assaults and the abbey bombing. The victory opened the Liri Valley and the road to Rome, but its cost made the campaign controversial.
details.casualties: Allied forces: about 55,000 casualties; Nazi Germany: about 20,000 killed or wounded

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Normandy: NormandySupply edit
image.attribution: The original uploader was MIckStephenson at English Wikipedia .

details.gloss: Allied landings and breakout operations opened a western front in Europe. Normandy made the liberation of France and the defeat of Germany a combined-front campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Leyte Gulf: USS Princeton (CVL 23) burning on 24 October 1944 (80 G 287970)

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Allied naval forces defeated Japan's attempt to disrupt the Philippines landings. Leyte Gulf is often considered the largest naval battle of World War II.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Iwo Jima: Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima, larger edit1

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US Marines captured a fortified volcanic island after intense fighting. The flag raising on Mount Suribachi became one of the war's most recognized images.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Okinawa: Ww2 158

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Okinawa was the last major battle of the Pacific War and caused enormous military and civilian losses. It shaped planning for a possible invasion of Japan.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Berlin: Raising a flag over the Reichstag Restoration

details.gloss: Soviet armies captured the German capital in the final European battle of World War II. Berlin's fall ended Nazi rule and divided the postwar city.
details.casualties: 1,057,665-1,286,367 (including civilians)

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Bulge: 117th Infantry North Carolina NG at St. Vith 1945

details.gloss: Germany launched a surprise winter offensive through the Ardennes. Allied resistance and counterattacks exhausted Germany's last major offensive in the west.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Manila: Manila Walled City Destruction May 1945

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Allied forces retook Manila in destructive urban combat against Japanese defenders. The battle devastated the city, caused catastrophic civilian losses, and marked the liberation of the Philippine capital.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dien Bien Phu: Victory in Battle of Dien Bien Phu

details.gloss: Viet Minh forces besieged and overran a French base in a remote valley. The defeat led to negotiations that ended French rule in Indochina.
details.casualties: 31,500, not including prisoners

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Inchon: Landing craft approaching Inchon, Korea, on 15 September 1950 (NH 42351)

details.gloss: MacArthur's amphibious landing at Inchon outflanked North Korean forces and recaptured Seoul. The operation reversed the early course of the Korean War.
details.casualties: United Nations Command: 224 killed and 809 wounded; North Korea: about 1,350 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chosin Reservoir: Chosin
image.attribution: Photo by Corporal Peter McDonald, USMC

details.gloss: Chinese forces surrounded UN troops in freezing mountain terrain, but the UN column fought its way out. Chosin became a defining battle of endurance.
details.casualties: United Nations Command: about 17,100 casualties including non-battle casualties; China: about 52,000 casualties including non-battle casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hue: Marines Fire From a House Window, February 1968 (16242259837) (cropped)

details.gloss: Communist forces seized much of Hue during the Tet Offensive, leading to weeks of urban combat. The battle changed American perceptions of the war.
details.casualties: US: about 250 killed and 1,554 wounded; North Vietnamese and Viet Cong: about 5,000 killed; civilian deaths also severe

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Goose Green: Remnants of 1982 Argentinian defensive positions Darwin Falklands

details.gloss: British paratroopers defeated Argentine forces at a key settlement after the San Carlos landings. Goose Green became one of the Falklands War's best-known ground battles.
details.casualties: British: 18 killed and 64 wounded; Argentine: about 45-55 killed, 86 wounded, and 961 captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Battle of Grozny: Evstafiev Chechnya BURNED

details.gloss: Russian forces captured Grozny after costly urban fighting and severe destruction. The battle exposed weaknesses in Russian planning and the brutality of city warfare.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Second Battle of Fallujah: 4 14 Marines in Fallujah
image.attribution: Lance Corporal Samantha L. Jones, USMC

combatants: United States and Iraqi government forces
details.gloss: Coalition forces retook Fallujah from insurgents in one of the Iraq War's fiercest urban battles. The operation became a reference point for modern city combat.
details.casualties: Coalition: about 107 killed and 613 wounded; insurgents: roughly 1,200-2,000 killed; civilian deaths disputed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mosul: Battle of Mosul Iraqi security forces transport combat equipment 2
image.attribution: U.S. Army photo by Maj. Damien Krantz

details.gloss: Iraqi forces and coalition partners retook Mosul after months of urban fighting. The victory ended Islamic State control of its largest Iraqi city.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tuyutí: Tuyuti

details.gloss: Paraguayan forces attacked the Triple Alliance camp but were repulsed with heavy losses. Tuyutí was one of the largest battles ever fought in South America.
details.casualties: Paraguay: 6,000 killed and 7,000 wounded; Triple Alliance: 996 killed and 3,232 wounded

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cuito Cuanavale: FAPLA tank over
image.attribution: The original uploader was Katangais at English Wikipedia..

details.outcome: Disputed tactical outcome; strategic pressure toward negotiated settlement
details.war: Angolan Civil War and South African Border War

series.label: Angolan Civil War and South African Border War

details.gloss: FAPLA, Cuban, South African, and UNITA forces fought around Cuito Cuanavale in a prolonged Cold War battle. Its legacy is disputed, but it shaped negotiations over Angola and Namibia.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Plataea: Scene of the Battle of Plataea

details.gloss: Greek hoplite armies defeated the Persian land force left in Greece after Salamis. Plataea ended Xerxes' invasion and confirmed Greek control of the mainland.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Hydaspes: Alexander the Great (356 23 BC) and Porus (oil on canvas)

details.war: Indian campaign of Alexander the Great
details.gloss: Alexander defeated King Porus after a difficult river crossing and battle against elephants. Hydaspes pushed Macedonian campaigning to its eastern limit.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chaeronea: Battle of Chaeronea, 338 BC en
image.attribution: Battle_of_Chaeronea,_338_BC.gif : Kirill Lokshin derivative work: Dipa_1965 ( talk )

details.gloss: Philip II defeated the allied Greek city-states and made Macedon the leading power in Greece. The battle set the stage for Alexander's later campaigns.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Issus: Battle of Issus mosaic Museo Archeologico Nazionale Naples 2013 05 16 16 25 06 BW

details.gloss: Alexander defeated Darius III in constricted terrain and captured the Persian royal camp. Issus proved that the Persian king himself could be beaten in battle.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ipsus: The Battle of Ipsus

details.gloss: The successors of Alexander defeated Antigonus, who died in battle. Ipsus fixed the division of Alexander's empire into durable Hellenistic kingdoms.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Metaurus: Mohammad adil rais battle of metaurus A

details.gloss: Roman forces defeated Hasdrubal Barca before he could join Hannibal in Italy. The victory removed Carthage's best chance to reinforce its Italian campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cynoscephalae: Macedonia and the Aegean World c.200

details.gloss: Roman legions defeated the Macedonian phalanx on broken ground. Cynoscephalae showed Rome's growing military influence in the Greek world.

image.alt: Map or diagram associated with Battle of Pydna: Plan battle of Pydna en

details.gloss: Rome defeated Perseus of Macedon and ended the Antigonid monarchy. Pydna completed Macedon's fall as an independent great power and confirmed Roman dominance in Greece.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Milvian Bridge: Battle of the Milvian Bridge by Giulio Romano, 1520 24

details.war: Roman civil wars of the Tetrarchy
details.gloss: Constantine defeated Maxentius outside Rome and became dominant in the western empire. The battle became famous for its association with Constantine's Christian vision.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Catalaunian Plains: Thuróczy krónika Attila király csatája a catalaunumi síkon

details.gloss: A Roman-led coalition checked Attila's advance in Gaul. The battle showed the late empire relying on federate alliances against new imperial powers.
details.casualties: 165,000 (ancient source)

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Badr: Siyer i Nebi Imam Ali und Hamza bei dem vorgezogenen Einzelkampf in Badr gegen die Götzendiener

details.gloss: Muhammad's followers defeated a larger Meccan force in the first major battle of the early Muslim community. Badr became central to Islamic memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Nahavand: Castle Nahavend by Eugène Flandin

details.gloss: Arab Muslim forces defeated a major Sasanian army in western Iran. Nahavand accelerated the disintegration of Sasanian resistance and became known as the victory of victories.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Khartoum: General Gordon's Last Stand

details.gloss: Mahdist forces captured Khartoum after a long siege and killed Charles Gordon. The fall shocked Britain and reshaped imperial policy in Sudan.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Covadonga: Invasionislámicaespaña

details.gloss: Asturian forces under Pelagius defeated a Muslim expedition in northern Iberia. Later tradition treated Covadonga as a symbolic beginning of the Reconquista.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dorylaeum: Dorylee2

details.gloss: Crusader forces survived a Seljuk attack and restored their march across Anatolia. Dorylaeum showed both the danger and resilience of the First Crusade.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Jerusalem: Taking of Jerusalem by the Crusaders, 15th July 1099

details.gloss: Crusader armies captured Jerusalem after a short siege and violent assault. The victory founded the Kingdom of Jerusalem and transformed crusading politics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Legnica: Battle of Legnica1241 From Legend of Saint Hedwig

details.gloss: A Mongol army defeated Duke Henry II and his allies in Silesia. Legnica demonstrated the reach of Mongol campaigns into central Europe.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Stirling Bridge: The Battle of Stirling Bridge

details.gloss: Scottish forces under William Wallace and Andrew Moray trapped the English at a narrow bridge. The victory revived Scottish resistance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bannockburn: Battle of Bannockburn

details.gloss: Robert Bruce defeated a larger English army near Stirling. Bannockburn secured Scottish momentum in the independence war and became a national landmark.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kosovo: Battle of Kosovo, Adam Stefanović, 1870

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Ottoman and Serbian-led armies fought a costly battle on Kosovo Field. Its political consequences and memory shaped Balkan history for centuries.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Nicopolis: NikopolisSchlacht

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Bayezid I defeated a large crusading army on the Danube. Nicopolis ended a major western attempt to halt Ottoman expansion in the Balkans.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Varna: Battle of Varna 1444

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Ottoman forces defeated a crusader army and King Wladyslaw III was killed. Varna strengthened Ottoman control before the fall of Constantinople.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Castillon: Français 5054, fol. 229v, Bataille de Castillon 1453 détail

details.gloss: French artillery and field defenses destroyed an English attack. Castillon effectively ended the Hundred Years War in France and showed the growing power of gunpowder defenses.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Diu: Insigne vitória alcançada pelo Vice Rei D. Francisco de Almeida contra as armadas do Sultão do Egito, e dos Reis de Cambaia e Calecute

details.war: Portuguese expansion in the Indian Ocean

series.label: Portuguese expansion in the Indian Ocean

details.gloss: A Portuguese fleet defeated a coalition near Diu and secured sea power in the western Indian Ocean. The battle reshaped Asian maritime trade routes.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cajamarca: La Captura de Atahualpa Juan Lepiani 1920s

details.war: Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire

series.label: Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire

details.gloss: Pizarro captured Atahualpa in a sudden attack at Cajamarca. The ambush destabilized the Inca Empire and opened the conquest of Peru.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Great Siege of Malta: Dimostrazione di tutte le batterie

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Ottoman forces failed to take Malta after a hard siege. The defense became a major symbol of Mediterranean resistance to Ottoman expansion.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Spanish Armada: Armadaroute

details.gloss: England disrupted Spain's invasion fleet through fireships, weather, and naval pressure. The Armada campaign became central to English maritime memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of White Mountain: The Battle of White Mountain (by Peter Snayers)

details.gloss: Imperial and Catholic League forces crushed the Bohemian revolt near Prague. White Mountain reshaped Bohemia and widened the Thirty Years War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Naseby: Battle of Naseby, hand coloured copper engraving

details.gloss: The New Model Army destroyed Charles I's main field army. Naseby made Parliament's victory in the first English Civil War likely and exposed the king's captured correspondence.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dunbar: Cromwell at Dunbar Andrew Carrick Gow (cropped)

details.gloss: Cromwell defeated a larger Scottish army after maneuvering out of a dangerous position. Dunbar opened Scotland to English occupation.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Poltava: Marten's Poltava

details.gloss: Peter the Great defeated Charles XII and broke Swedish offensive power. Poltava marked Russia's rise as a major European military state.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Quebec: Benjamin West 005

details.gloss: British troops scaled the heights near Quebec and defeated Montcalm on the Plains of Abraham. The victory helped decide control of New France.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Buxar: Sir Hector Munro by David Martin

details.war: British East India Company expansion in India

series.label: British East India Company expansion in India

details.gloss: Company forces defeated the Nawab of Awadh, the Nawab of Bengal, and the Mughal emperor. Buxar consolidated British revenue power in Bengal.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battles of Saratoga: Surrender of General Burgoyne

details.gloss: American forces forced Burgoyne's army to surrender in upstate New York. Saratoga helped bring France openly into the war and turned the Revolution into a global conflict.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Culloden: The Battle of Culloden

details.gloss: Government forces defeated the Jacobite army in a short, destructive battle. Culloden ended the last major Jacobite attempt to restore the Stuart line.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Marengo: Lejeune Bataille de Marengo

details.gloss: Napoleon recovered from near defeat and beat the Austrians in northern Italy. Marengo secured his political standing as First Consul.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Jena-Auerstedt: Iena

details.gloss: French forces defeated Prussia in twin battles on the same day. Jena-Auerstedt shattered the old Prussian army and transformed German politics.
details.casualties: 52,000, including prisoners later killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Friedland: Napoleon friedland

details.gloss: Napoleon defeated Bennigsen's Russian army near Friedland. The victory led to the Treaties of Tilsit and a temporary Franco-Russian settlement.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of New Orleans: Battle of New Orleans, Jean Hyacinthe de Laclotte
image.attribution: Painting by Jean Hyacinthe de Laclotte (1766 - 1829), a member of the Louisiana Militia who participated in the battle; painted by him after the victory based on his sketches made at the scene.

details.gloss: Andrew Jackson's forces repelled a British attack after the peace treaty had been signed but before news arrived. The victory boosted American national confidence.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chacabuco: Battle of Chacabuco

details.gloss: San Martin's Army of the Andes defeated royalists after crossing from Argentina into Chile. Chacabuco opened the road to Santiago and revived the Chilean independence cause.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Maipu: BatallaDeMaipu

details.gloss: Patriot forces defeated a royalist army near Santiago. Maipu secured Chilean independence after the crisis following Cancha Rayada.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Boyaca: Batalla de Boyaca de Martin Tovar y Tovar

details.gloss: Bolivar's army defeated royalist forces and captured the road to Bogota. Boyaca secured New Granada for the independence movement and strengthened Gran Colombia.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Carabobo: BatallaCarabobo01

details.gloss: Bolivar's forces defeated the main royalist army in Venezuela. Carabobo made Spanish control of the region untenable and secured a major step toward Gran Colombia.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Puebla: Batalla de Puebla, 5 5 1862 (1870)

details.gloss: Mexican forces under Ignacio Zaragoza repelled a French assault on Puebla. The victory became a symbol of resistance celebrated as Cinco de Mayo.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chapultepec: Nebel Mexican War 10 Chapultepec Pillow (cropped)

details.gloss: US forces stormed Chapultepec Castle during the campaign for Mexico City. The victory opened the capital and became famous in US Marine Corps memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Balaclava: Charge of the Light Brigade, Balaclava, 25 October 1854

combatants: British Empire, France, and Ottoman Empire
details.gloss: Allied forces held the port of Balaclava against Russian attack. The battle is remembered for the Charge of the Light Brigade and the confusion of Crimean War command.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Sedan: Nap sedan von Wilhelm Camphausen

details.gloss: German forces encircled and captured Napoleon III's army. Sedan collapsed the Second Empire, transformed the Franco-Prussian War, and cleared the way for German unification.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Little Bighorn: Charles Marion Russell The Custer Fight (1903)

details.gloss: Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho warriors destroyed Custer's battalion. The victory became iconic but brought intensified US campaigns.
details.casualties: Lakota, Northern Cheyenne, and Arapaho: about 31-136 killed; United States: 268 dead and 55 wounded

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Rorke's Drift: Alphonse de Neuville The defence of Rorke's Drift 1879 Google Art Project

details.gloss: A small British garrison held a mission station against repeated Zulu attacks. Rorke's Drift became famous for its defense after Isandlwana.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Blood River: Bloedrivier monument

details.gloss: Voortrekker commandos defeated a Zulu army from a fortified wagon laager. The battle became central to Afrikaner nationalist memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Manila Bay: Battle of Manila Bay by James Gale Tyler (1898)

details.gloss: Dewey's squadron destroyed the Spanish fleet in Manila Bay. The victory brought the United States into Philippine imperial politics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of San Juan Hill: San Juan Hill by Kurz and Allison

details.gloss: US and Cuban forces captured heights outside Santiago de Cuba. The battle became famous through the Rough Riders and Theodore Roosevelt.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Spion Kop: Boers at Spion Kop, 1900 Project Gutenberg eText 16462
image.attribution: Boers at Spion Kop, 1900 - Project Gutenberg eText 16462

details.gloss: Boer forces repelled a British attempt to seize a key hill during the relief of Ladysmith. Spion Kop exposed British tactical failures.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tanga: Battle of tanga

details.gloss: German colonial forces under Paul von Lettow-Vorbeck defeated a larger British landing at Tanga. The battle embarrassed Britain and shaped the early East African campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Jutland: HMS Warspite and HMS Malaya during the battle of Jutland

details.outcome: British strategic advantage; German tactical survival
details.gloss: The main British and German battle fleets clashed in the North Sea. Jutland was indecisive tactically but left British naval blockade strategy intact.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cambrai: Bundesarchiv Bild 104 0941A, Bei Cambrai, zerstörter englischer Panzer Mark I

details.gloss: British forces used tanks in a large coordinated attack against the Hindenburg Line. Cambrai showed both promise and limits of armored breakthrough.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Belleau Wood: US Army Infantrymen, 2nd Infantry Division, Battle of Belleau Wood, June 1918

details.gloss: US Marines and Army troops fought German forces in close terrain near the Marne. Belleau Wood became a defining American memory of World War I.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Warsaw: Battle of Warsaw 1920

details.gloss: Polish forces counterattacked and stopped the Soviet advance on Warsaw. The victory preserved Polish independence and checked westward Bolshevik momentum.
details.casualties: 130,000, approximately, not including prisoners

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battles of Khalkhin Gol: Japanese soldiers creeping in front of wrecked Soviet tanks

details.gloss: Zhukov defeated Japanese forces on the Mongolian frontier with armor, artillery, and air power. The defeat influenced Japan's strategic turn southward.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Britain: Spitfire and He 111 during Battle of Britain 1940

details.gloss: The Royal Air Force defeated Germany's attempt to gain air superiority over Britain. The battle prevented invasion and made air defense decisive.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Attack on Pearl Harbor: Attack on Pearl Harbor Japanese planes view

details.outcome: Japanese tactical victory; American entry into war

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Japanese carrier aircraft struck the US Pacific Fleet at Pearl Harbor. The attack brought the United States into World War II and made carrier air power central to the Pacific War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Coral Sea: Large explosion aboard USS Lexington (CV 2), 8 May 1942 (80 G 16651)

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Carrier aircraft fought while the opposing surface fleets never sighted each other. Coral Sea checked Japan's move toward Port Moresby and foreshadowed carrier warfare at Midway.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kasserine Pass: Kasserine Pass

details.gloss: German and Italian forces mauled inexperienced American units in Tunisia. Kasserine forced rapid Allied changes in command and tactics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tarawa: Marines Advance on Japanese Pill Boxes, Tarawa, November 1943 (10962107133)

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US Marines captured Betio after a costly amphibious assault against fortified Japanese defenses. Tarawa taught hard lessons in landing craft, naval fire, and reef operations for later Pacific campaigns.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Saipan: Marines and King Kong on Saipan

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US forces captured Saipan, putting Japan within range of B-29 bombers. The defeat triggered a political crisis in Tokyo and brought the home islands under sustained air attack.
details.casualties: 43,500 killed, including mass suicides

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Peleliu: First wave of LVTs moves toward the invasion beaches Peleliu

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US Marines and Army troops fought entrenched Japanese defenders in coral ridges and caves. Peleliu became controversial for its cost and limited strategic value.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Operation Market Garden: Waves of paratroops land in Holland
image.attribution: This file is a work of a U.S. Army soldier or employee, taken or made as part of that person's official duties. As a work of the U.S. federal government , it is in the public domain in the United States.

details.gloss: Allied airborne and ground forces tried to seize bridges into Germany. Market Garden failed at Arnhem and left the war in the west unresolved.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hurtgen Forest: HurtgenForest

details.gloss: American forces fought through dense forest, mines, and fortifications near the German border. Hurtgen became one of the US Army's costliest campaigns in Europe.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Imphal: Gurkhas advancing with Lee tanks to clear the Japanese from Imphal Kohima road
image.attribution: British Information Service, information service operated by UK government during WW2.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Slim's Fourteenth Army defeated a Japanese invasion attempt in northeastern India. Imphal, with Kohima, turned the Burma campaign and ended Japan's hopes of breaking into India.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kohima: IND 003698 Garrison Hill Kohima
image.attribution: Official photographer of No.9 Army Film and Photographic Unit.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Fighting around Kohima Ridge stopped Japan's advance toward India. The battle became famous for close combat around the tennis court.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Crete: Bundesarchiv Bild 141 0864, Kreta, Landung von Fallschirmjägern

details.gloss: German paratroopers captured Crete after heavy losses. The operation proved airborne forces could seize an island but at a severe cost.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kiev: Bundesarchiv Bild 183 B12190, Kiew, Brand in der Hauptstraße

details.gloss: German forces encircled huge Soviet armies around Kyiv. The victory was one of the largest encirclements of the war but delayed the drive on Moscow.
details.casualties: 761,783 killed, captured, and missing

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Guam: First flag on Guam 1944

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US Marines and Army troops recaptured Guam after weeks of fighting. The island became a major B-29 and naval base for the final campaigns against Japan.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Wake Island: USMC M Wake 17

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: American defenders repelled the first Japanese landing attempt before surrendering to a second attack. Wake became an early Pacific War symbol of resistance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River: U.S. Army machine gun team near the Chongchon River in North Korea (November 1950)
image.attribution: U.S. Army machine gun team near the Chongchon River in North Korea (November 1950).jpg

details.gloss: Chinese forces launched a major counteroffensive against UN armies in North Korea. The battle drove UN forces south and transformed the Korean War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Khe Sanh: Khe Sanh Bunkers and burning Fuel Dump
image.attribution: Khe Sanh Bunkers and burning Fuel Dump

details.outcome: American and South Vietnamese defensive victory
details.gloss: North Vietnamese forces besieged the US Marine base at Khe Sanh during the Tet period. The battle became a test of firepower and logistics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ia Drang: Bruce Crandall's UH 1D

details.gloss: US air cavalry fought North Vietnamese regulars in the first major US-NVA battle. Ia Drang previewed the war's reliance on helicopters and firepower.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Longewala: Tank tracks during the Battle of Longewala in 1971

details.gloss: A small Indian post held against a Pakistani armored attack until air support arrived. Longewala became a famous Indian defensive victory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of 73 Easting: Type 69 Operation Desert Storm

details.gloss: Coalition armored units destroyed Iraqi Republican Guard elements in the desert. 73 Easting became a model case of modern armored warfare.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mogadishu: Black Hawk Down Super64 over Mogadishu coast
image.attribution: The original description page was here . All following user names refer to en.wikipedia.

details.outcome: Somali tactical success; US withdrawal pressure
details.gloss: A US raid turned into a prolonged urban battle after helicopters were shot down. Mogadishu changed American and UN policy in Somalia.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tora Bora: Meeting between Haji Zaman Gamarshareek, Hazret Ali, CIA Juliet Team leader "George", during Tora Bora battle

details.gloss: US and Afghan forces attacked al-Qaeda positions in the mountains near Tora Bora. The battle became controversial because Osama bin Laden escaped.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Marawi: Marawi crisis troops

details.gloss: Philippine forces retook Marawi after months of urban combat against militants. The battle exposed the danger of Islamic State-linked insurgency in Southeast Asia.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Raqqa: SDF fighters in Raqqa downtown

details.gloss: US-backed Syrian Democratic Forces captured Islamic State's de facto capital after a destructive urban battle. Raqqa marked a major defeat for the caliphate project.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Kobani: Coalition Airstrike on ISIL position in Kobane
image.attribution: Voice of America News: Scott Bobb reports from the Suruç, Turkey/ Kobane, Syrian border; "Turkish Border Towns Hosting Thousands of Kobani Refugees".

details.gloss: Kurdish defenders held Kobani against Islamic State assault with coalition air support. The siege became a symbolic turning point in the fight against ISIS.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Basra: 1 IG cover sappers capping a burning oil well. Basra, Iraq. 03 04 2003 MOD 45142639

details.gloss: British forces captured Basra during the invasion of Iraq after fighting around the city and airport. Control of Basra secured a key southern port and oil region.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Algiers: Casbah cache Ali lapointe

details.gloss: French forces dismantled FLN networks in Algiers through raids, intelligence, and torture. The battle won the city tactically but damaged France politically.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of El Guettar: American soldiers hands out cigarettes to captured Italians of the Bersaglieri Division near El Guettar, Tunisia. NARA 196343
image.attribution: Unknown author Unknown author or not provided

details.gloss: US forces repelled German armored attacks in Tunisia after the lessons of Kasserine. El Guettar showed improving American battlefield performance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the River Plate: HMS Achilles (70)

details.location: South Atlantic near Uruguay and Argentina

series.label: World War II Atlantic naval war

details.gloss: British cruisers forced the German raider Admiral Graf Spee into Montevideo, where it was scuttled. The battle was an early Allied naval success.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Milne Bay: Australian troops at Milne Bay

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Australian and American forces defeated a Japanese landing at Milne Bay. It was one of the first clear Allied land victories against Japanese troops.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Kokoda Track Campaign: Australian 39th Battalion after the Kokoda Track campaign 1942 (AWM 013289)

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Australian forces fought a difficult jungle campaign to stop Japan's advance toward Port Moresby. Kokoda became central to Australian wartime memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Savo Island: USS Quincy (CA 39) under fire during the Battle of Savo Island on 9 August 1942 (NH 50346)
image.attribution: Imperial Japanese Navy, copied from the Rear Admiral Samuel Eliot Morison World War II history illustrations file. Originally uploaded to English Wikipedia [1] by user Stan Shebs .

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Japanese cruisers surprised and defeated Allied warships near Guadalcanal. Savo Island was one of the US Navy's worst surface defeats.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Salamaua-Lae campaign: Battle of salamaua
image.attribution: Taken by Lt. Sidney Seff 6th Photo Tech Sq. US Army

details.location: Lae and Salamaua, Papua New Guinea

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Allied operations captured Salamaua and Lae through coordinated landings and overland advances. The campaign pushed Japan back in New Guinea.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Buna-Gona: Wounded Australian soldier led by a Papuan orderly at Buna

details.location: Buna and Gona, Papua New Guinea

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Australian and American troops fought entrenched Japanese positions on the north coast of New Guinea. Buna-Gona was a costly Allied victory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Riachuelo: Palácio Pedro Ernesto Batalha do Riachuelo cópia
image.attribution: Oscar Pereira da Silva / After Victor Meirelles

details.gloss: Brazilian warships defeated Paraguay on the Parana River. Riachuelo secured Allied river control during the Paraguayan War and protected the supply route into Paraguay.
details.casualties: Paraguay: about 750 casualties; Brazil: 104 killed, 123 wounded, and 20 missing

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kambula: Kambula 1

details.gloss: British forces defeated Zulu attacks from a prepared defensive position. Kambula helped restore British confidence after Isandlwana.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Majuba Hill: Battle of Majuba Hill

details.gloss: Boer marksmen defeated British troops on Majuba Hill. The battle helped force a settlement recognizing Transvaal self-government.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ohaeawai: The ohaeawai stockade

details.gloss: British troops failed against a fortified Maori pa at Ohaeawai. The battle demonstrated the sophistication of Maori defensive engineering.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Gate Pa: Attack at Gate Pā
image.attribution: Archibald Forbes , Arthur Griffiths and others.

details.gloss: Maori defenders at Gate Pa repelled a much larger British assault. The battle became a notable defeat for imperial forces in New Zealand.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Fei River: Battle of Fei River

details.gloss: Eastern Jin forces routed a much larger Former Qin army. The defeat fractured Former Qin power and preserved southern Chinese independence.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Siffin: An Illustrated and Illuminated leaf from a Persian Manuscript, Persia, Safavid, dated 1516

details.gloss: The armies of Ali and Muawiya fought during the first major Islamic civil war. Siffin deepened the political and religious divisions of the early caliphate.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Guadalete: The battle of Guadelete

details.gloss: Umayyad-led forces defeated the Visigothic army of King Roderic. The battle opened the rapid Muslim conquest of most of Iberia and transformed western Mediterranean history.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Stamford Bridge: Battle of Stamford Bridge, full

details.gloss: Harold Godwinson defeated Harald Hardrada and Tostig Godwinson in northern England. The victory ended the Norwegian invasion but left England exposed before Hastings.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Myriokephalon: Crusades surprised by turks

details.gloss: Seljuk forces ambushed a Byzantine army in Anatolia. The battle damaged Byzantine hopes of restoring control over the interior of Asia Minor.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Arsuf: Schlacht von Arsuf

details.gloss: Richard I defeated Saladin near Arsuf during the Third Crusade. The victory restored Crusader morale and secured the coastal march toward Jaffa.

title: Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa: Batalla de las Navas de Tolosa, por Francisco van Halen

details.location: Near Las Navas de Tolosa, Spain
details.gloss: A coalition of Iberian kingdoms defeated the Almohad army in Andalusia. The battle shifted the long-term balance of power in the Reconquista.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Crecy: Battle of crecy froissart

details.gloss: English longbowmen and defensive positioning defeated repeated French attacks. Crecy became a landmark battle in the Hundred Years War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Poitiers: Battle poitiers(1356)

details.gloss: Edward the Black Prince defeated a larger French army and captured King John II. Poitiers intensified the political crisis in France.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Lake Poyang: 明祖鄱陽滅友諒（廿一史通俗衍義）

details.gloss: Zhu Yuanzhang defeated Chen Youliang in a vast naval battle. The victory cleared Zhu's path toward founding the Ming dynasty and deciding the Red Turban struggle.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ankara: Timur reviews his troops in the plain of Sivas in the battle of Ankara in mid summer 1402. Zafarnama of 1436, facing folios 356r (left), 355v (right)

details.gloss: Timur defeated and captured Ottoman sultan Bayezid I. Ankara plunged the Ottoman state into an interregnum and delayed its expansion.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Lutzen: Death of King Gustav II Adolf of Sweden at the Battle of Lützen (Carl Wahlbom) Nationalmuseum 18031

details.gloss: Swedish forces defeated an Imperial army, but Gustavus Adolphus was killed. The battle preserved Swedish influence while changing the war leadership.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Nordlingen: Jan van der Hoecke The Battle of Nördlingen, 1634

details.gloss: Imperial and Spanish forces crushed the Protestant field army. Nordlingen reversed Swedish momentum and pushed France toward direct intervention.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Fontenoy: Pierre Lenfant La Bataille de Fontenoy, le 11 mai 1745 (Château de Versailles)

details.gloss: French forces defeated an Allied army in the Austrian Netherlands. Fontenoy became one of France's major eighteenth-century battlefield victories.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Leuthen: Battle of Leuthen 2

details.gloss: Frederick the Great used an oblique attack to defeat a larger Austrian army. Leuthen is often treated as one of his finest victories.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Wandiwash: Bataille de Vandavachy gagnée par les Anglais sur les Français le 22 janvier 1760 communiqué par d'Estaing btv1b532541002

details.gloss: British forces defeated the French in southern India during the Carnatic struggle. Wandiwash helped end French hopes of matching British power in India.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Third Battle of Panipat: The Third battle of Panipat 13 January 1761

details.gloss: Ahmad Shah Durrani defeated the Maratha Confederacy in a huge and costly battle. Panipat reshaped power politics across northern India.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Assaye: Battle of Assaye
image.attribution: Joseph Constantine Stadler / After William Heath

details.gloss: Arthur Wellesley defeated a larger Maratha army in western India. Assaye strengthened Company control and built Wellesley's reputation before his later fame as Wellington.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Shiloh: Thure de Thulstrup Battle of Shiloh

details.gloss: Union forces survived a surprise Confederate attack and counterattacked the next day. Shiloh revealed how costly the Civil War would become.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Antietam: Thure de Thulstrup Battle of Antietam

details.gloss: Antietam halted Lee's invasion of Maryland after the bloodiest single day of the war. The result gave Lincoln space to issue the Emancipation Proclamation.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Second Battle of the Marne: Western front 1918 german

details.gloss: Allied forces stopped Germany's final major offensive and began the counteroffensive phase of 1918. The battle marked a decisive turn toward Allied victory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Nanking: Attacking the Gate of China02

details.gloss: Japanese forces captured the Chinese capital after hard fighting. The battle was followed by the Nanjing Massacre and became central to wartime memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Wuhan: Wuhan 1938

details.gloss: Chinese forces fought a long defense around Wuhan before withdrawing. The battle slowed Japan and moved the war into a prolonged phase.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Suomussalmi: Battle suomussalmi

details.gloss: Finnish troops destroyed Soviet columns in deep winter conditions. Suomussalmi became a classic example of mobility, terrain, and small-unit tactics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Leningrad: Anti aircraft Leningrad 1941

details.gloss: Axis forces encircled Leningrad for nearly 900 days, causing mass civilian starvation. The city endured until Soviet offensives broke the siege.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Brody: Bundesarchiv B 145 Bild F016199 10, Zerstörter sowjetischer Panzer

details.gloss: Soviet mechanized corps counterattacked German armor in one of the largest tank battles of 1941. The defeat exposed early Soviet command and coordination problems.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Bismarck Sea: Japanese transport under attack during the Battle of the Bismarck Sea, 3 March 1943 (128159)

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Allied aircraft destroyed a Japanese convoy bound for New Guinea. The battle sharply limited Japan's ability to reinforce the theater.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Philippine Sea: Japanese aircraft carrier Zuikaku and two destroyers under attack on 20 June 1944 (80 G 238025)

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US carrier forces defeated a major Japanese fleet sortie near the Marianas. The battle crippled Japanese naval aviation and became known as the Great Marianas Turkey Shoot.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Seelow Heights: Gedenkstätte Seelower Höhen Blick ins Oderbruch

details.gloss: Soviet armies broke through German defenses east of Berlin under Zhukov and Konev. Seelow Heights opened the final assault on the German capital.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Long Tan: Australian soldier on the Long Tan battlefield on 19 August 1966
image.attribution: Not stated on the AWM database

details.gloss: Australian and New Zealand troops fought a much larger force in a rubber plantation. Long Tan became one of Australia's best-known Vietnam War battles.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Maling: Battle of Maling

details.gloss: Qi forces used deception and ambush to defeat Wei. Maling became a classic Chinese example of stratagem and battlefield intelligence.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Leuctra: Pelopidas leading the Thebans at the battle of Leuctra

details.gloss: Epaminondas and the Thebans broke Spartan battlefield dominance with an innovative deep formation. Leuctra reshaped Greek politics and military tactics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mantinea: Bartolomeo Pinelli La Morte di Epaminonda 1812

details.gloss: Epaminondas won at Mantinea but was killed in the fighting. The battle ended the brief peak of Theban power and left Greece without a stable hegemon.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Granicus: Charles Le Brun, Le Passage du Granique, 1665

details.gloss: Alexander crossed into Asia and defeated Persian satrapal forces at the Granicus. The victory opened the Achaemenid Empire to his campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Trebia: Battle of Trebbia (M. Merian) (cropped)

details.gloss: Hannibal lured Roman forces into a cold river crossing and enveloped them. Trebia confirmed the danger of his Italian invasion and began a run of Carthaginian victories.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Lake Trasimene: Sylvestre Ducar decapite Flaminius (Trasimene)

details.gloss: Hannibal ambushed a Roman army beside Lake Trasimene. It became one of the largest successful ambushes in ancient warfare and deepened Rome's crisis in Italy.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Changping: Battle of Changping

details.gloss: Qin defeated Zhao in a catastrophic campaign that broke a major rival. Changping accelerated Qin's movement toward unifying China and became infamous for mass killing.
details.casualties: 700,000 (ancient source & includes prisoners)

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Guandu: Guanduzhizhan eng
image.attribution: Guanduzhizhan.png : User Zhuwq on zh.wikipedia derivative work: Deadkid dk ( talk ) 02:40, 10 July 2009 (UTC)

details.war: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty

series.label: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty

details.gloss: Cao Cao defeated Yuan Shao despite being outnumbered. Guandu set the balance of power in northern China before the Three Kingdoms and showed Cao Cao's strategic skill.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Edessa: Naghsh e rostam, Irán, 2016 09 24, DD 12

details.gloss: Shapur I defeated the Romans and captured Emperor Valerian. Edessa became one of Rome's most humiliating imperial defeats and advertised Sasanian power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Naissus: GothicInvasions 267 269 en

details.war: Gothic invasions of the Roman Empire
details.gloss: Roman forces crushed a major Gothic invasion in the Balkans. The victory helped stabilize the empire during a period of severe crisis.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Strasbourg: Battle of Argentoratum

details.gloss: Julian defeated a larger Alamannic force near Strasbourg. The battle restored Roman authority along part of the Rhine frontier and enhanced Julian's military reputation.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dara: Battle of Dara battleplan

details.gloss: Belisarius used fieldworks and maneuver to defeat a Sasanian army. Dara became one of the best-known early Byzantine victories and displayed Justinian's revived eastern defenses.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hulao: Battle of Luoyang Hulao

details.gloss: Li Shimin defeated rival claimants at Hulao Pass during the Tang struggle for supremacy. The victory helped secure Tang rule and enhanced Li Shimin's reputation before he became Emperor Taizong.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ajnadayn: Mohammad adil Muslims Invasion of Syria

details.gloss: Rashidun forces defeated a Byzantine field army in Palestine. Ajnadayn opened the way for further Muslim advances in the Levant before Yarmouk decided the campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kleidion: SamuilsDeathBGhistory

details.gloss: Basil II crushed the Bulgarian army at Kleidion after a long Byzantine-Bulgarian struggle. The victory broke Bulgarian resistance and helped bring the empire under Byzantine control.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Antioch: SiegeofAntioch

details.gloss: Crusaders endured a long siege and then a counter-siege at Antioch. The victory kept the First Crusade alive on the road to Jerusalem.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ascalon: 1099 Ascalon

details.gloss: Crusader forces defeated a Fatimid army soon after the capture of Jerusalem. Ascalon secured the first wave of crusader conquests and protected the newborn Kingdom of Jerusalem.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Montgisard: Schlacht von Montgisard 2

details.gloss: A small Crusader army under Baldwin IV surprised and defeated Saladin. Montgisard temporarily checked Ayyubid pressure on Jerusalem.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Acre: Philippe Auguste et Richard Acre

details.gloss: The long siege of Acre became the central operation of the Third Crusade. Its capture gave the Crusaders a coastal base and drew leaders such as Richard I and Philip II into the campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bouvines: Bataille de Bouvines gagnee par Philippe Auguste

details.gloss: Philip II defeated a coalition of enemies at Bouvines. The victory strengthened Capetian monarchy and weakened King John's position.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Baghdad: Baghdad 1258

details.war: Mongol conquest of the Abbasid Caliphate
details.gloss: Mongol forces captured Baghdad and ended the Abbasid caliphate political power. The siege became a symbol of catastrophic urban destruction.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Golden Spurs: Battle of Courtrai2

details.gloss: Flemish infantry defeated French cavalry near Kortrijk. The battle challenged assumptions about knightly dominance and became a founding memory for Flemish civic resistance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Sluys: BattleofSluys

details.gloss: English ships destroyed much of the French fleet at Sluys. The naval victory gave England control of Channel movement early in the war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kulikovo: Dmitry Donskoy in the Battle of Kulikovo

details.gloss: Dmitry Donskoy defeated Mamai's forces at Kulikovo. The battle became central to Russian memory of resistance to Horde power, even though Mongol influence continued.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Orleans: SiegeOfOrleans1429
