details.gloss: French forces relieved Orleans after Joan of Arc's arrival transformed morale. The victory reversed momentum in the later Hundred Years War and led toward Charles VII's coronation.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bosworth Field: Battle of Bosworth by Philip James de Loutherbourg

details.gloss: Henry Tudor defeated Richard III, who was killed on the field. Bosworth ended Plantagenet rule, began the Tudor dynasty, and closed the main phase of the Wars of the Roses.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Flodden: Flodden Memorial geograph.org.uk 39370

details.gloss: English forces defeated the Scots and killed King James IV. Flodden became one of Scotland's most severe military disasters and reshaped Anglo-Scottish politics.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Khanwa: Babur’s army in battle against the army of Rana Sanga at
image.attribution: "Mirza 'Abd al-Rahim, Khan-i khanan (Mirza 'Abd al-Rahim, Khan-i khanan)"

details.gloss: Babur defeated Rana Sanga's Rajput-led coalition soon after Panipat. Khanwa proved the Mughals could survive beyond a single victory and helped secure their hold in northern India.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Fourth Battle of Kawanakajima: BattleKawanakajima
image.attribution: after Katsukawa Shuntei (died in 1824) by Hiroshige (1797-1858)

details.gloss: The fourth battle of Kawanakajima became the most famous clash between Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin. Its drama made it a fixture of Sengoku memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Talikota: Battle of Raksas Tagdi 01

details.gloss: The Deccan sultanates defeated Vijayanagara and devastated its capital region. Talikota marked a major shift in South Indian power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Haldighati: Chokha, Battle of Haldighati, painted 1822

details.gloss: Mughal forces fought Rana Pratap's army in the Aravalli hills. Though tactically indecisive, Haldighati became a powerful symbol of Rajput resistance to Mughal expansion.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Myeongnyang: Navalzhugenu2

details.gloss: Admiral Yi Sun-sin defeated a much larger Japanese fleet in a narrow strait. Myeongnyang is one of Korea's most celebrated naval victories.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Noryang: 『征倭紀功図巻』に描かれた露梁海戦

details.gloss: Allied Korean and Ming forces struck the retreating Japanese fleet. Noryang ended the naval phase of the Imjin War and cost Yi Sun-sin his life.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Edgehill: Harvey, Prince of Wales and Duke of York Wellcome M0016114 (cropped)

details.gloss: The first major pitched battle of the English Civil War failed to decide the conflict. Edgehill showed that the war would not end quickly.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ramillies: De slag bij Ramillies tussen de Fransen en de Geallieerden, 23 mei 1706, SK A 4061

details.gloss: Marlborough defeated the French army in the Spanish Netherlands. Ramillies opened much of the region to Allied occupation and confirmed his reputation after Blenheim.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Malplaquet: Copy after Jan van Huchtenburgh (Haarlem 1647 Amsterdam 1733) The Battle of Malplaquet, 1709 RCIN 404898 Royal Collection

details.gloss: Marlborough and Eugene forced the French from the field but suffered heavy losses. Malplaquet showed the rising cost of the War of the Spanish Succession and blunted Allied momentum.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Rossbach: Schlacht bei Roßbach

details.gloss: Frederick the Great crushed a larger Franco-Imperial army by maneuver. Rossbach became one of Prussia's most famous victories and boosted Frederick's European prestige.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kunersdorf: Alexander von Kotzebue Battle of Kunersdorf on 1 August 1759

details.gloss: Russian and Austrian forces nearly destroyed Frederick the Great's army. Kunersdorf brought Prussia close to disaster and exposed how fragile Frederick's position was in the Seven Years War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bunker Hill: The Death of General Warren at the Battle of Bunker's Hill

details.gloss: British troops took the heights near Boston but suffered heavy losses. Bunker Hill proved colonial forces would stand against regulars and hardened the Revolutionary War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Trenton: Battle of Trenton by Charles McBarron
image.attribution: Published by U.S. Government Printing Office; painting by Hugh Charles McBarron, Jr. (1902-1992)

details.gloss: Washington crossed the Delaware and surprised the Hessian garrison at Trenton. The victory revived American morale after a difficult campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cowpens: The Battle of Cowpens by Don Troiani

details.gloss: Daniel Morgan used militia and regulars in a layered defense to defeat Banastre Tarleton. Cowpens damaged British strength in the southern campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Guilford Court House: Battle of Guilford Courthouse 15 March 1781
image.attribution: H. Charles McBarron , The original uploader was Oldwildbill at English Wikipedia .

details.gloss: Cornwallis won the field but took heavy losses against Nathanael Greene. Guilford Court House weakened the British southern army before the Yorktown campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Eylau: Gros, Napoleon at Eylau

details.gloss: French and Russian armies fought a brutal winter battle with no decisive result. Eylau challenged Napoleon's image of effortless victory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Aspern-Essling: Archduke Charles, Duke of Teschen, with his Staff at the Battle of Aspern Essling, 21–22 May 1809 (by Johann Peter Krafft, 1820)

details.gloss: Archduke Charles defeated Napoleon after the French crossed the Danube. Aspern-Essling was Napoleon's first major battlefield defeat.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Wagram: Napoleon Wagram

details.gloss: Napoleon defeated Archduke Charles in a massive two-day battle near Vienna. Wagram ended the War of the Fifth Coalition, though its cost showed Napoleon's wars becoming harder to win cleanly.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Salamanca: The 88th Foot at the Battle of Salamanca, 1812

details.gloss: Wellington exploited a French deployment error and won a sharp victory in Spain. Salamanca opened the road to Madrid and proved that French armies could be beaten in open battle.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ligny: Battle of Ligny

details.gloss: Napoleon defeated Blucher Prussian army two days before Waterloo. The victory was not decisive enough to prevent Prussian intervention later.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Quatre Bras: The Prince of Orange at Quatre Bras

details.gloss: Wellington blocked Ney at Quatre Bras while the Prussians fought at Ligny. The battle shaped the movements before Waterloo and helped keep Allied armies from being separated.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Navarino: Navarino

details.gloss: British, French, and Russian fleets destroyed the Ottoman-Egyptian fleet at Navarino. The naval victory helped secure Greek independence and was the last major battle fought entirely by sailing warships.

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Battle of Bull Run: Bridge during the Retreat from Manassas, First Battle of Bull Run (First Manassas), Virginia 1861

details.gloss: The first major battle of the Civil War ended in a Confederate victory near Manassas. Bull Run shattered expectations of a short war and forced both sides to prepare for a longer conflict.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Fredericksburg: Gallant Charge of Humphrey's Division at the Battle of Fredericksburg LCCN2004661366 (cropped)

details.gloss: Union assaults failed against strong Confederate positions on Marye Heights. Fredericksburg became a symbol of costly frontal attack.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chancellorsville: Schlacht bei Chancellorsville 2

details.gloss: Lee divided his army and defeated a larger Union force under Hooker. Chancellorsville was his greatest victory but cost Stonewall Jackson, making it strategically bittersweet.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chickamauga: Unbekannt Confederate General Hood wounded at the battle of Chickamauga American Civil War (MeisterDrucke 739711)

details.gloss: Confederate forces routed much of the Union Army of the Cumberland in northern Georgia. Chickamauga was the Confederacy's major western victory but failed to recover Chattanooga.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Atlanta: Georgia, Atlanta, Confederate Works Southeast NARA 533414

details.gloss: Union forces defeated Confederate attacks east of Atlanta during Sherman's campaign. The fall of Atlanta helped secure Lincoln's reelection.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Plevna: Grivita 1877

details.gloss: Ottoman defenses at Plevna delayed the Russian advance for months. The siege demonstrated the power of modern entrenched firepower.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ulundi: The burning of Ulundi (cropped)

details.gloss: British forces defeated the Zulu army near the royal kraal at Ulundi. The battle effectively ended organized Zulu resistance in the war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mons: 4th Bn Royal Fusiliers 22 August 1914
image.attribution: This photograph Q 70071 comes from the collections of the Imperial War Museums .

details.gloss: The British Expeditionary Force fought its first major battle against Germany at Mons. The battle began the long British retreat from Belgium.

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Battle of Ypres: A Photo of German Soldiers During the Battle of Ypres 352029188390

details.gloss: Allied forces stopped German attempts to break through around Ypres. The battle helped establish the trench line of the Western Front.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Second Battle of Ypres: RJB18 – Ypernbogen
image.attribution: Unknown author Unknown author - Author wrote the Story but he used and didn't create the photos or pictures before

details.gloss: German forces used poison gas on a large scale near Ypres. The battle introduced a terrifying new weapon to the Western Front and hardened the war's industrial character.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Gorlice-Tarnow offensive: Bitwa pod Gorlicami (1 H 34 3)

details.gloss: German and Austro-Hungarian forces broke through Russian lines in Galicia. Gorlice-Tarnow forced a major Russian retreat and restored Central Powers momentum in the east.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Brusilov offensive: Defenders NGM v31 p369 A

details.gloss: Brusilov offensive shattered Austro-Hungarian armies and drew in German reserves. It was Russia's most successful operation of the war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of France: Bundesarchiv Bild 101I 054 1531 11, Frankreich, erste Hilfe für Verwundeten

details.gloss: Germany defeated France and the Allied armies in a rapid campaign built around armored breakthroughs. The fall of France transformed World War II and left Britain fighting on alone.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battles of Narvik: Allied soldiers during the battle of Narvik

details.outcome: Allied local victory; German campaign success
details.gloss: Naval and land battles around Narvik contested control of northern Norway. Allied forces won locally but withdrew after the wider German campaign succeeded.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Tobruk: Soldiers of the Allied Armies in Tobruk 1941

details.gloss: Allied defenders held Tobruk against Rommel's Axis siege for months. The defense disrupted Axis operations in North Africa and made the garrison a symbol of resistance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Gazala: Bundesarchiv Bild 101I 784 0246 22A, Nordafrika, Rommel im Befehlsfahrzeug "Greif"

details.gloss: Rommel defeated the Eighth Army around Gazala and captured Tobruk soon after. The battle opened the Axis advance toward Egypt before El Alamein halted it.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Changsha: IJA, Battle of Changsha, China, September 1939

details.gloss: Chinese forces repelled a Japanese attempt to take Changsha. The victory demonstrated that Japan could still be stopped in major inland operations.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Changde: Changde battle

details.gloss: Chinese troops fought a costly defense of Changde against Japanese forces. The battle became one of the major Chinese urban fights of the war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Anzio: Landing at Anzio

details.gloss: Allied forces landed at Anzio to outflank German defenses in Italy but became pinned near the beachhead. The battle ended with the breakout toward Rome.
details.casualties: 52,200 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Operation Bagration: Soviet soldiers in Polozk (Belarus), passing by propaganda poster celebrating the reconquest of the city and urging the liberation of the Baltic from Nazi German occupation. July 4, 1944

details.gloss: Soviet armies destroyed German Army Group Centre in a massive offensive. Bagration was one of Germany's greatest defeats of the war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Falaise pocket: St. Lambert surrender
image.attribution: Donald I. Grant / Canada. Dept. of National Defence / Library and Archives Canada / PA-116586Original uploader was Climie.ca at en.wikipedia

details.gloss: Allied armies encircled large German forces during the breakout from Normandy. The Falaise pocket helped end the German position in France.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Aegospotami: Battle of Aegospotami

details.gloss: Spartan forces destroyed the Athenian fleet at Aegospotami. The battle cut Athens off from grain supplies and led directly to its surrender.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Amphipolis: Thucydides, Maps and Plans illustrative of Wellcome L0031928

details.gloss: Brasidas defeated an Athenian force near Amphipolis, and both Brasidas and Cleon were killed. The battle helped create the conditions for the Peace of Nicias.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Raphia: Battle raphia

details.gloss: Ptolemy IV defeated Antiochus III in one of the largest battles of the Hellenistic age. Raphia preserved Ptolemaic control of Coele-Syria for a time.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Magnesia: Battle of Magnesia

details.gloss: Roman and allied forces defeated Antiochus III at Magnesia. The battle confirmed Roman power in the eastern Mediterranean and weakened the Seleucid Empire.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Aquae Sextiae: John Harris Valda The Battle of Aquae Sextiae in 102 BC

details.gloss: Gaius Marius defeated the Teutones and Ambrones in southern Gaul. The victory helped end the immediate threat of large northern migrations into Roman territory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Vercellae: Tiepolo Vercellae

details.gloss: Marius and Catulus destroyed the Cimbri at Vercellae. The battle ended one of the gravest threats Rome had faced since Hannibal and raised Marius' political prestige.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Pharsalus: Battle of Pharsalus, 48 BC
image.attribution: The Department of History, United States Military Academy

details.gloss: Caesar defeated Pompey in Greece despite being outnumbered. Pharsalus made Caesar the dominant figure in the Roman civil war and sent Pompey fleeing to Egypt.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Munda: Battle of Munda (M. Merian)

details.gloss: Caesar defeated the last major Pompeian army at Munda. The battle ended organized resistance to his rule in the Roman world and left the dictatorship uncontested.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Philippi: Philippi location
image.attribution: No machine-readable author provided. Marsyas assumed (based on copyright claims).

details.gloss: Antony and Octavian defeated Brutus and Cassius at Philippi. The battle ended the republican faction that had killed Caesar and pushed Rome toward triumviral rule.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Salsu: Documentary painting of the Battle of Salsu
image.attribution: War Memorial of Korea Open Archives

details.gloss: Goguryeo forces destroyed a huge Sui army during its retreat. Salsu became one of Korea's most famous victories and weakened the Sui dynasty.
details.casualties: 302,300 (ancient source)

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Nineveh: Histoire de la guerre sainte de Guilaume de Tyr BNF Fr2824 f1r (bataille de Ninive)

details.gloss: Heraclius defeated the Sasanian army near Nineveh after a bold winter campaign. The victory helped force Persia to make peace with Byzantium.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Fahl: 20100921 pella08

details.gloss: Rashidun forces defeated Byzantine troops near Fahl after earlier victories in Palestine. The battle further weakened Byzantine control in the southern Levant.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Jerusalem: Otto Clemens Fikentscher Omar's arrival to Jerusalem (engraving)

details.gloss: Jerusalem surrendered to Rashidun forces after a siege during the Muslim conquest of the Levant. The city transfer became one of the most important events of the early Islamic conquests.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Camel: Ali and Aisha at the Battle of the Camel

combatants: Forces of Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr
details.gloss: Ali forces defeated opponents led by Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr near Basra. The battle opened the first major civil war within the Muslim community.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Karbala: Brooklyn Museum Battle of Karbala Abbas Al Musavi cropped

details.gloss: Umayyad forces killed Husayn ibn Ali and his small party at Karbala. The battle became central to Shia memory and made martyrdom a defining theme in Islamic history.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Constantinople: 47 cropped manasses chronicle
image.attribution: 47-manasses-chronicle.jpg : Original: Constantine Manasses derivative work: Stegop

details.gloss: Byzantine defenders held Constantinople against a major Umayyad siege by land and sea. The victory preserved Byzantium and checked Umayyad expansion toward southeastern Europe.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dandanaqan: Gaznaly seljuk Dandanaqan

details.gloss: Seljuk forces defeated the Ghaznavids near Merv. Dandanaqan opened the way for Seljuk dominance across Iran and the wider Middle East.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Civitate: Battaglia di Civitate 18 06 1053

details.gloss: Norman forces defeated a papal-led army at Civitate and captured Pope Leo IX. The battle confirmed Norman power in southern Italy and pushed the papacy toward accommodation.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Didgori: Didgori Monument (Swords)

details.gloss: David IV of Georgia defeated a much larger Seljuk-led coalition at Didgori. The victory helped launch the Georgian Golden Age and became central to Georgian national memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Alarcos: Alarcos (Ciudad Real) castillo (RPS 25 08 2012)

details.gloss: Almohad forces defeated Castile at Alarcos. The defeat delayed Castilian expansion until the Christian coalition victory at Las Navas de Tolosa.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kose Dag: Bataille de Közä Dagh (1243)

details.gloss: Mongol forces defeated the Sultanate of Rum at Kose Dag. The battle reduced Seljuk power in Anatolia and reshaped the region political future.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of La Forbie: La Forbie

details.gloss: Crusader and allied Syrian forces were crushed near Gaza at La Forbie. The defeat badly weakened the military strength of the Crusader states.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mansurah: Mansura

details.gloss: Egyptian forces defeated the crusading army of Louis IX around Mansurah. The battle led to the capture of the French king and the failure of the Seventh Crusade.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Worringen: Battle of Worringen 1288

details.gloss: Brabant and its allies defeated Guelders in the War of the Limburg Succession. Worringen shaped power in the lower Rhine and Low Countries.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Halidon Hill: Charge of the Scots at Halidon Hill

details.gloss: English forces defeated a Scottish army attacking uphill at Halidon Hill. The battle showed the deadly combination of archers and defensive ground.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Nevilles Cross: BNMsFr2643FroissartFol97vBatNevilleCross

details.gloss: English forces defeated and captured King David II of Scotland near Durham. The battle weakened Scotland during a critical phase of the Hundred Years War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Formigny: Formigny

details.gloss: French forces used artillery and cavalry to defeat an English army in Normandy. Formigny helped end English control of Normandy and pointed toward the closing phase of the Hundred Years War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tewkesbury: MS Ghent Battle of Tewkesbury
image.attribution: Tewkesbury1.jpg : Unknown derivative work: Jappalang ( talk )

details.gloss: Yorkist forces defeated the Lancastrians at Tewkesbury and killed or captured much of their leadership. The battle secured Edward IV return to power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chaldiran: A painting in Chehel Sotoun1

details.gloss: Ottoman forces defeated the Safavids with artillery and firearms at Chaldiran. The battle shaped the Ottoman-Safavid frontier and sectarian politics of the region.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Marj Dabiq: Mercidabık Muharebesi2

details.gloss: Selim I defeated the Mamluks at Marj Dabiq, opening Syria to Ottoman conquest. The battle began the collapse of Mamluk power and shifted the eastern Mediterranean balance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Rhodes: OttomanJanissariesAndDefendingKnightsOfStJohnSiegeOfRhodes1522
image.attribution: Fethullah Çelebi Arifi (historian, poet and painter) and/or Matrakçı Nasuh (painter of landscape) and/or other painters at the court of Sultan Suleiman the Magnificent

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Ottoman forces captured Rhodes after a major siege against the Knights Hospitaller. The victory removed a key Christian naval base in the eastern Mediterranean.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Preveza: Battle of Preveza (1538)
image.attribution: Osman Nuri Pasha and Hovhannes Umed Behzad (1809–1874)

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Barbarossa defeated a Holy League fleet near Preveza. The battle confirmed Ottoman naval strength in the eastern Mediterranean before Lepanto.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Szigetvar: Johann Peter Krafft 005

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Suleiman forces captured Szigetvar after a fierce defense by Nikola Zrinski. The siege delayed the Ottoman advance and became legendary in Hungarian and Croatian memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ivry: Henri IV à la bataille d'Ivry

details.gloss: Henry IV defeated the Catholic League at Ivry during his fight for the French crown. The victory strengthened his claim and became linked to his famous white-plume legend.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kinsale: Kinsale 1601 02

details.gloss: English forces defeated Irish and Spanish allies at Kinsale. The battle broke the main Gaelic resistance to Tudor conquest in Ireland.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Marston Moor: Battle of Marston Moor 1644 by John Barker

details.gloss: Parliamentarian and Scottish Covenanter forces defeated the Royalists at Marston Moor. The battle secured northern England for Parliament.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Worcester: Cromwell at the Battle of Worcester, 1651

details.gloss: Cromwell defeated Charles II royalist army at Worcester. The battle ended the main military phase of the Wars of the Three Kingdoms.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Four Days Battle: The 'Royal Prince' and other Vessels at the Four Days Battle, 1–4 June 1666 RMG BHC0286

details.gloss: Dutch and English fleets fought for four days in one of the longest naval battles of the age of sail. The Dutch victory showed the continuing strength of their fleet.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Boyne: De slag aan de Boyne (Ierland) tussen Jacobus II en Willem III, 12 juli 1690 Rijksmuseum SK A 605

details.gloss: William III defeated James II's forces at the Boyne. The battle became a defining event in Irish and British political memory and secured Williamite control.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Zenta: A zentai csata Eisenhut Ferenc képe

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Prince Eugene surprised and destroyed an Ottoman army crossing the Tisza at Zenta. The victory helped force the Treaty of Karlowitz.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Oudenarde: Copy after Jan van Huchtenburgh (Haarlem 1647 Amsterdam 1733) The Battle of Oudenarde, 1708. RCIN 404900 Royal Collection

details.gloss: Marlborough and Eugene defeated a French army at Oudenarde. The victory opened the way to the siege of Lille and kept France under pressure in the Spanish Succession war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Minden: The battle of Minden (cropped)

details.gloss: An Allied army defeated French forces at Minden during the Seven Years War. The battle checked French power in western Germany and became famous for the advance of British infantry.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Porto Novo: Battle of Porto Novo

details.gloss: Company forces under Eyre Coote defeated Hyder Ali at Porto Novo. The battle checked Mysore momentum in the Second Anglo-Mysore War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Valmy: Valmy Battle painting

details.gloss: French forces stopped the Prussian advance at Valmy. The battle helped save the Revolution and gave confidence to the new French Republic.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bailen: La Rendición de Bailén (Casado del Alisal)

details.gloss: Spanish forces forced a French corps to surrender at Bailen. The defeat shattered Napoleon's aura of invincibility in Spain and encouraged wider resistance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Vitoria: George Jones (1786 1869) The Battle of Vittoria RCIN 407186 Royal Collection

details.gloss: Wellington defeated Joseph Bonaparte army at Vitoria. The battle broke French power in Spain and pushed the war toward the Pyrenees.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bladensburg: Final Stand at Bladensburg

details.gloss: British troops routed American defenders at Bladensburg. The victory opened the way to Washington, D.C., where British forces burned public buildings.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Horseshoe Bend: Battle of Tohopeka

details.gloss: Andrew Jackson's forces defeated the Red Stick Creeks at Horseshoe Bend. The battle ended the Creek War and forced major land cessions.
details.casualties: United States and allies: fewer than 50 killed and 154 wounded; Red Stick Creeks: roughly 800 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Alamo: 1854 Alamo

details.gloss: Mexican forces stormed the Alamo after a thirteen-day siege. The defeat became a rallying symbol for Texian independence and fed the cry remembered at San Jacinto.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of San Jacinto: The Battle of San Jacinto (1895)

details.gloss: Sam Houston's army surprised and defeated Santa Anna at San Jacinto. The battle effectively secured Texas independence from Mexico in a fight lasting less than twenty minutes.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cerro Gordo: Nebel Mexican War 05 Battle of Cerro Gordo (cropped)

details.gloss: Winfield Scott defeated Santa Anna at Cerro Gordo during the advance from Veracruz. The victory opened the route toward Mexico City.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Inkerman: C. Faure Bataille d'Inkerman Charge du 26e de ligne britannique (cropped)

details.gloss: Allied troops defeated a Russian attack near Inkerman in confused foggy fighting. The battle helped secure the siege lines around Sevastopol.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Wilderness: Genl. U.S. Grant at Wilderness LCCN2004661535 (cropped)

details.gloss: Grant and Lee fought in dense Virginia woodland at the Wilderness. The battle began the relentless Overland Campaign toward Richmond and Petersburg.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cold Harbor: The soldier in our Civil War a pictorial history of the conflict, 1861 1865, illustrating the valor of the soldier as displayed on the battle field, from sketches drawn by Forbes, Waud, Taylor, (14576591097)

details.gloss: Union assaults failed against entrenched Confederate positions at Cold Harbor. The battle became one of the stark examples of the cost of frontal attacks in 1864.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Petersburg: Dictatorcrop

details.gloss: Union forces besieged Petersburg for months, stretching Confederate defenses around Richmond. The fall of Petersburg forced Lee retreat and led to Appomattox.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cerro Corá: CHICO DIABO atravessando com uma lança Solano López (Semana Illustrada n 485, 27.03.1870)

details.gloss: Brazilian forces killed Paraguayan president Francisco Solano Lopez at Cerro Cora. The battle ended the Paraguayan War and left Paraguay devastated.
details.casualties: Paraguay: 100 killed and 240 captured; Brazil: 7 wounded

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Angamos: Angamos5

details.gloss: Chile captured the Peruvian ironclad Huascar at Angamos. The battle gave Chile naval dominance in the War of the Pacific and opened the way for coastal operations.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Miraflores: El tercer reducto

details.gloss: Chilean forces defeated Peruvian defenders at Miraflores after fighting outside Lima. The victory led to the occupation of the Peruvian capital.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Loos: Jagger Cambrai

details.gloss: British forces attacked at Loos and used poison gas for the first time. The battle produced heavy casualties for limited gains and exposed problems in British command and supply.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Vimy Ridge: Richard Jack The Taking of Vimy Ridge (CWM 19710261 0160)

details.gloss: Canadian forces captured Vimy Ridge during the Arras offensive through careful planning and artillery coordination. The battle became a central moment in Canadian military memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Vittorio Veneto: Battle of Vittorio Veneto

details.gloss: Italian and Allied forces broke the Austro-Hungarian army at Vittorio Veneto. The battle hastened Austria-Hungary collapse at the end of World War I.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hong Kong: Battle of HK 03

combatants: British, Canadian, Indian, and local defenders

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Japanese forces captured Hong Kong after a short but hard campaign in December 1941. The battle was part of Japan's opening offensive across the Pacific and Asia.
details.casualties: 18,000 including prisoners

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Java Sea: Haguro 1942

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Japanese warships defeated an Allied squadron attempting to defend the Dutch East Indies. The battle opened the way for Japan's conquest of Java.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Dieppe Raid: Bundesarchiv Bild 101I 362 2211 04, Dieppe, Landungsversuch, englischer Spähpanzer

details.gloss: Allied forces, mostly Canadian, suffered heavy losses in a raid on Dieppe. The failure influenced planning for later amphibious operations.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Aachen: GI machine gun crew in Aachen (Correct orientation)

details.gloss: US forces captured Aachen after intense urban combat on the German frontier. It was the first major German city taken by the western Allies and previewed the cost of fighting into the Reich.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Budapest: Russian Soldier Budapest

details.gloss: Soviet and Romanian forces besieged and captured Budapest after brutal urban fighting. The siege destroyed much of the city and weakened Axis defenses in Hungary.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Lake Balaton: Bundesarchiv Bild 146 1989 105 13A, Ungarn, deutscher Rückzug

details.gloss: Germany launched its last major offensive near Lake Balaton in Hungary. Soviet forces stopped the attack and resumed the drive toward Vienna.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Abu-Ageila: Flickr Government Press Office (GPO) Centurion Tanks

details.gloss: Israeli forces broke through Egyptian defenses at Abu-Ageila in a coordinated night attack. The victory opened the route across Sinai during the Six-Day War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Chinese Farm: Destroyed tank of the Egyptian army on the Sinai, 1973 (2)

details.location: Sinai Peninsula near the Suez Canal
details.gloss: Israeli and Egyptian forces fought around the Chinese Farm during Israel's counteroffensive in Sinai. The battle helped secure Israel's bridgehead across the Suez Canal.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Muye: Battle of Muye

details.gloss: The Zhou army defeated the Shang near Muye and overthrew the last Shang king. The battle became a foundational episode in Chinese dynastic history.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chengpu: Battle of Chengpu

details.gloss: Jin forces defeated Chu at Chengpu after a carefully staged withdrawal. The victory established Duke Wen of Jin as a leading hegemon.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Guiling: Battle of Guiling

details.gloss: Qi used a feint against Wei's home territory to relieve pressure on Zhao. The battle became a classic example of attacking an enemy's vulnerable center of gravity.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Julu: 巨鹿之戰 邢台博物館

details.gloss: Xiang Yu destroyed major Qin forces at Julu during the rebellions against Qin rule. The victory accelerated the dynasty's collapse.
details.casualties: 400,000+ (including prisoners)

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Allia: Servian Wall Termini Station

details.gloss: A Gallic army routed Roman forces near the Allia River. The defeat opened the way for the sack of Rome and left a deep mark on Roman memory and military reform.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Sentinum: Carte TroisGuerreSamnite 298avJC

details.gloss: Rome defeated a coalition of Samnites, Gauls, and other enemies at Sentinum. The battle helped secure Roman dominance in central Italy and close the Third Samnite War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Heraclea: Pyrrhic War Italy en

details.gloss: Pyrrhus defeated a Roman army using Hellenistic tactics and war elephants. The battle introduced Rome to a formidable eastern Mediterranean style of warfare.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Asculum: Pyrrhic War Italy en

details.gloss: Pyrrhus won again at Asculum but at severe cost against Rome. The battle gave rise to the idea of a victory so costly it undermines the victor.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Beneventum: Beneventum battle

details.gloss: Roman forces checked Pyrrhus at Beneventum and ended his Italian campaign. The result strengthened Rome's path toward control of southern Italy.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mycale: Priene colline colonne
image.attribution: No machine-readable author provided. Pedro Lassouras assumed (based on copyright claims).

details.gloss: Greek forces destroyed Persian naval power at Mycale on the same campaign cycle as Plataea. The victory helped shift the war into the Aegean and Asia Minor.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Eurymedon: Eurymedon Bridge, Aspendos, Turkey. Pic 01

details.gloss: Cimon won a combined land and sea victory over Persian forces near the Eurymedon. The battle confirmed Athenian-led pressure in the eastern Mediterranean.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Sphacteria: Battle of sphacteria

details.gloss: Athens captured Spartan hoplites on Sphacteria after fighting near Pylos. The surrender shocked the Greek world and shifted bargaining power during the Peloponnesian War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Delium: Socrates at the Battle of Delium (Thomas Couture, 1843)

details.gloss: Boeotian forces defeated an Athenian army near Delium. The battle showed Athens' limits on land even at the height of its imperial resources.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Syracuse: Destruction of the Athenian army at Syracuse

details.gloss: Athens sent a vast expedition to capture Syracuse and lost its fleet and army. The disaster weakened Athens for the final phase of the Peloponnesian War.

image.alt: Map or diagram associated with Battle of Arginusae: Battle of Arginusae New Map
image.attribution: Leonidas1206, CC0, via Wikimedia Commons; modified as described by GreekMaps

details.gloss: Athens won a major naval victory at Arginusae but failed to rescue many survivors. The political aftermath damaged Athenian command at a critical moment.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Gabiene: Eumenes of Cardia

details.gloss: Antigonus defeated Eumenes after a hard-fought successor battle in the east. The result removed one of the last major loyalists to Alexander's royal house.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Sellasia: SouthernPeloponessus

details.gloss: Macedonian and Achaean forces defeated reforming Sparta at Sellasia. The battle ended Cleomenes III's attempt to restore Spartan power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ilipa: Ilipa2

details.gloss: Scipio Africanus defeated the main Carthaginian army in Hispania at Ilipa. The victory cleared the way for Rome to attack Carthaginian power directly in Africa.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Baecula: Iberia 210 206BC it
image.attribution: Iberia 237-206BC.svg : Alcides Pinto File:Iberia 237-206BC-fr.svg : Cj73 derivative work: Cristiano64 ( talk )

details.gloss: Scipio defeated Hasdrubal Barca at Baecula during the struggle for Hispania. The victory weakened Carthaginian control but did not stop Hasdrubal from marching toward Italy.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mylae: The story of the greatest nations, from the dawn of history to the twentieth century a comprehensive history, founded upon the leading authorities, including a complete chronology of the world, and (14578958927)
image.attribution: Ellis, Edward Sylvester, 1840-1916; Horne, Charles F. (Charles Francis), 1870-1942

details.gloss: Rome won its first major naval victory against Carthage at Mylae. The battle showed that Roman boarding tactics could offset Carthaginian seamanship.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cape Ecnomus: The Naval Battle Near Ecnomus (256 BC)

details.gloss: Roman and Carthaginian fleets fought one of antiquity's largest naval battles near Cape Ecnomus. Rome's victory opened the way for an invasion of Africa.

image.alt: Map or diagram associated with Battle of Drepana: Battle of drepana map
image.attribution: Muriel Gottrop , תרגום על ידי he:משתמש:Ingsoc

details.gloss: Carthaginian ships destroyed a Roman fleet at Drepana. The defeat was Rome's worst naval setback of the First Punic War and delayed its command of the sea.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Arausio: Cimbrians and Teutons invasions

details.gloss: Cimbri and Teutones destroyed Roman forces near Arausio after divided Roman commanders failed to cooperate. The disaster helped pave the way for Marius' military reforms.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Colline Gate: The British Library Rome Porta Collina

details.gloss: Sulla defeated Marian and Samnite forces at the gates of Rome. The battle secured his dictatorship and transformed Roman politics through proscriptions and constitutional changes.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tigranocerta: Hpa tigranakertbattle69

details.gloss: Lucullus defeated Tigranes the Great near Tigranocerta despite being heavily outnumbered. The battle exposed the vulnerability of Armenia's expanded empire.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Watling Street: Schlacht an der Watling Street, schematisch

details.gloss: A smaller Roman army crushed Boudica's rebel forces somewhere along Watling Street. The victory secured Roman control in Britain after a major uprising.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Jerusalem: (Venice) La distruzione del tempio di Gerusalemme Francesco Hayez gallerie Accademia Venice

details.gloss: Roman forces under Titus captured Jerusalem after a devastating siege. The destruction of the Second Temple reshaped Jewish and Roman history.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mons Graupius: Agricola.Campaigns.80.84

details.gloss: Tacitus describes Agricola defeating Caledonian forces at Mons Graupius. The battle marked the high-water point of Roman campaigning in northern Britain.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Abritus: 10 2023 Palazzo Altemps, Roma, Lazio, 00186, Italia Sarcofago Grande Ludovisi (Grande Ludovisi sarcophagus) Arte Romana Photo Paolo Villa FO232047 ombre gimp bis

details.war: Gothic invasions of the Roman Empire
details.gloss: Gothic forces defeated the Romans at Abritus, killing Emperor Decius. The battle exposed the empire's vulnerability during the third-century crisis.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Frigidus: Janez Vajkard Valvasor Bitka med Teodozijem in Evgenijem

details.gloss: Theodosius defeated Eugenius and Arbogast near the Frigidus River. The battle reunited the empire briefly and strengthened Nicene Christian imperial policy.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Uhud: The Prophet Muhammad and the Muslim Army at the Battle of Uhud, from the Siyer i Nebi, 1595

details.gloss: Quraysh forces checked the Muslims near Mount Uhud after part of the Muslim line abandoned its position. The battle became a major lesson in discipline and resilience in early Islamic memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Trench: Battle of Trench 627 CE

details.gloss: Medina's defenders used a trench to block a confederate siege. The failure of the siege strengthened Muhammad's position in Arabia.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Zab: Zab rivers

details.gloss: Abbasid forces defeated the Umayyad army near the Zab River. The battle ended Umayyad rule in the central caliphate and opened the Abbasid era.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Constantinople: 42 manasses chronicle

details.gloss: Constantinople withstood a combined Avar and Sasanian threat while Heraclius campaigned elsewhere. The failed siege preserved the Byzantine capital at a moment of extreme danger.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Pliska: 52 manasses chronicle

details.gloss: Bulgar forces destroyed a Byzantine army after Emperor Nikephoros I sacked Pliska. The emperor was killed, making the battle a severe Byzantine disaster.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Anchialus: Battle of Anchialos (917) es
image.attribution: Battle_of_Anchialos_(917).svg : Todor Bozhinov derivative work: Rowanwindwhistler ( talk ) translator: Manlleus (ca)

details.gloss: Simeon I's Bulgarians crushed a Byzantine army near Anchialus. The battle confirmed Bulgaria as a dominant Balkan power during Simeon's imperial ambitions.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bach Dang: Five Dynasties Ten Kingdoms 923 CE

details.gloss: Ngo Quyen used stakes in the Bach Dang River to destroy a Southern Han fleet. The battle became a landmark in Vietnamese independence.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Clontarf: 'Battle of Clontarf', oil on canvas painting by Hugh Frazer, 1826

details.gloss: Brian Boru's forces defeated Leinster and Norse-allied opponents near Dublin. Brian was killed, but the battle became central to Irish historical memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Brunanburh: Athelstan

details.gloss: Athelstan defeated a northern coalition at Brunanburh. The victory helped secure the idea of a unified English kingdom and became celebrated in Old English verse.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Maldon: Alfred Pearseː Battle of Maldon in 991 (Hutchinson's Story of the British Nation, 1922)

details.gloss: Viking raiders defeated an Anglo-Saxon force led by Byrhtnoth at Maldon. The battle became famous through an Old English poem about loyalty and defeat.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dyrrhachium: Bataille de Dyrrachion (1081) BnF Latin 4915

details.gloss: Robert Guiscard defeated Alexios I Komnenos near Dyrrhachium. The battle opened a dangerous Norman invasion of Byzantine territory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Harran: Harranruins2
image.attribution: No machine-readable author provided. Gerry Lynch~commonswiki assumed (based on copyright claims).

details.gloss: Seljuk forces defeated an army from Edessa and Antioch near Harran. The battle checked early crusader expansion beyond northern Syria.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Beroia: Jean II Comnene

details.gloss: John II Komnenos defeated the Pechenegs near Beroia. The victory ended the Pechenegs as a major threat to Byzantium and strengthened Komnenian recovery in the Balkans.

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Battle of Tarain: Prithvi Raj Chauhan (Edited)
image.attribution: Prithvi_Raj_Chauhan.JPG : LRBurdak at en.wikipedia derivative work: original uploader was Dhiresh b at en.wikipedia

details.gloss: Prithviraj Chauhan defeated Muhammad of Ghor near Tarain in 1191. The victory temporarily checked Ghurid expansion into northern India.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Second Battle of Tarain: The last stand of Rajputs against Muhammadans

details.gloss: Muhammad of Ghor returned to Tarain and defeated Prithviraj Chauhan. The battle opened the way for lasting Muslim-ruled polities in northern India.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chandawar: Photograph of an Indian miniature painting depicting Muhammad of Ghor of the Ghurid Dynasty, published in 'Tawarikh i Ghuri' by Munshi Bulaqi Das Sahib (1881)

details.gloss: Ghurid forces defeated Jayachandra of the Gahadavala dynasty at Chandawar. The battle extended Ghurid control into the Ganges plain.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Kalka River: MongolCavalrymen

details.gloss: A Mongol reconnaissance army destroyed a Rus-Cuman coalition on the Kalka River. The battle foreshadowed the larger Mongol invasion of Rus lands.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Indus: Jalal al Din Khwarazm Shah crossing the rapid Indus river, escaping Chinggis Khan and his army

details.war: Mongol conquest of the Khwarazmian Empire
details.gloss: Genghis Khan defeated Jalal al-Din near the Indus after the collapse of Khwarazm. Jalal escaped, but Mongol dominance in Central Asia was confirmed.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Yehuling: Bataille entre mongols & chinois (1211)

details.war: Mongol conquest of the Jin dynasty
details.gloss: Genghis Khan's army defeated a much larger Jin force at Yehuling. The battle opened northern China to sustained Mongol pressure and began the fall of Jin power.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Kiev: Siege of Kiev (1240)
image.attribution: Anonymous Russian manuscript illuminators, 1560-1570s Facial Chronicle (Illustrated Chronicle of Ivan the Terrible) (in 10 volumes: pdf , pdf with translation ) Public domain image

details.gloss: Mongol forces stormed Kiev after a siege in 1240. The city's fall symbolized the devastation of the Mongol invasion of Rus and shifted power away from old Kievan centers.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Xiangyang: Trebuchet2

details.gloss: Mongol forces captured Xiangyang and Fancheng after a long siege aided by counterweight trebuchets. The victory opened the route into the Song heartland.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dan-no-ura: AntokuTennou Engi.7&8 Dannoura Kassen

details.gloss: The Minamoto defeated the Taira in a decisive naval battle at Dan-no-ura. The result ended the Genpei War and helped establish the Kamakura shogunate.

image.alt: Map or diagram associated with Battle of Baekgang: Map of the Battle of Baekgang

details.gloss: Tang and Silla forces defeated Baekje loyalists and their Yamato allies at Baekgang. The battle ended hopes of restoring Baekje and reshaped power on the Korean peninsula.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bun'ei: Mooko HakataWall

details.gloss: Mongol Yuan forces landed near Hakata Bay during the first invasion of Japan. The expedition withdrew after fierce fighting and severe weather.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Koan: Mōko Shūrai Ekotoba e18

details.gloss: A huge Yuan invasion force failed to break Japanese defenses and was wrecked by storms. The battle became central to the kamikaze tradition in Japanese memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Falkirk: 208 of '(The Pictorial History of Scotland ... A.D. 79 1746. By J. Taylor, assisted by Professor Lindsay, Professor Eadie, J. Anderson, G. Macdonald, and other contributors.)' (11170063244)

details.gloss: Edward I defeated William Wallace's army at Falkirk. English longbowmen and cavalry broke the Scottish schiltrons and ended Wallace's command.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Morgarten: Bendicht Tschachtlan, Die Schlacht am Morgarten (c. 1470)

details.gloss: Swiss forces ambushed a Habsburg army at Morgarten. The battle strengthened the early Swiss Confederation's military reputation and became part of Swiss foundation memory.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Maritsa: BattleofMaritsa1371

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: Ottoman forces surprised and defeated Serbian armies near the Maritsa River. The victory accelerated Ottoman expansion into the Balkans.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Blue Waters: Stamp 2012 Bytva Syni Vody (1)

details.gloss: Lithuanian forces defeated Golden Horde armies at Blue Waters. The battle expanded Lithuanian influence across parts of Ukraine and weakened steppe control in the region.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cerignola: Elgrancapitantrasbatalladeceriñola

details.gloss: Spanish forces defeated a French army at Cerignola using field defenses and firearms. The battle is often cited as an early sign of gunpowder infantry's importance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ravenna: 1512 Ravenne

details.gloss: French and Ferrarese forces defeated the Holy League near Ravenna but suffered heavy losses. The battle showed the destructive power of artillery and pike warfare.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Otumba: Battle of Otumba (Conquest of Mexico) Painting

details.war: Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire

series.label: Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire

details.gloss: Cortes' battered army defeated Aztec forces at Otumba after fleeing Tenochtitlan. The victory allowed the Spaniards and their allies to recover and continue the conquest.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Orsha: Autor nieznany (malarz z kręgu Lukasa Cranacha Starszego), Bitwa pod Orszą

combatants: Grand Duchy of Lithuania and Poland
details.gloss: Lithuanian and Polish forces defeated a Muscovite army near Orsha. The battle checked Muscovy's advance after the loss of Smolensk.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Diu: Primeiro cerco de Diu. Solimão, General otomano, é constrangido a levantá lo, e pôr se em fuga

series.label: Portuguese expansion in the Indian Ocean

details.gloss: Portuguese defenders held Diu against a major Ottoman-Gujarati siege. The defense preserved Portuguese naval positions in the western Indian Ocean.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Gravelines: Battle of Gravelines, second stage Wellcome M0012866

details.gloss: English ships attacked the Spanish Armada near Gravelines and disrupted its formation. The battle helped prevent Spain's planned invasion of England.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Nieuwpoort: Prins Maurits in de Slag bij Nieuwpoort, 2 juli 1600, SK A 664

details.gloss: Maurice of Nassau defeated Spanish forces near Nieuwpoort. The battle demonstrated Dutch tactical reforms even though the campaign gained little strategic ground.
details.casualties: Dutch Republic and England: about 1,700-2,700 dead or wounded; Spain: about 4,000 killed, wounded, or captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Jankau: Old copper engraving of the Battle of Jankov

details.gloss: Swedish forces defeated an imperial army at Jankau and opened the road toward Vienna. The battle intensified pressure for a negotiated end to the Thirty Years War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Saint Gotthard: Mogersdorf1

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

details.gloss: A Habsburg-led army defeated Ottoman forces near Saint Gotthard. The victory checked Ottoman pressure in western Hungary and shaped the uneasy peace that followed.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of La Hogue: Benjamin West, The Battle of La Hogue, c. 1778, NGA 45885 (cropped)

details.gloss: Anglo-Dutch forces defeated the French fleet around Barfleur and La Hogue. The battle ended immediate French hopes of invading England for James II.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cartagena de Indias: Defensa de Cartagena de Indias por la escuadra de D. Blas de Lezo, año 1741

details.gloss: Spanish defenders repelled a large British expedition against Cartagena de Indias. The failed assault preserved Spanish control of a key Caribbean port.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dettingen: John Wootton (c.1682 1764) George II at the Battle of Dettingen, with the Duke of Cumberland and Robert, 4th Earl of Holderness, 27 June 1743 NAM. 1961 07 116 National Army Museum

details.gloss: George II was present when the Pragmatic Army defeated French forces at Dettingen. It was the last battle in which a British monarch personally led troops.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hohenfriedberg: Hohenfriedeberg Attack of Prussian Infantry 1745

details.gloss: Frederick the Great defeated an Austro-Saxon army at Hohenfriedberg. The victory confirmed Prussia's battlefield strength in the Silesian wars.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Prague: Battle of Prague, 6 May 1757 Attempted envelopment

details.gloss: Frederick the Great defeated Austrian forces outside Prague but suffered heavy losses. The victory led to a siege that was soon undone by defeat at Kolin.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kolin: Schlacht Kolin 1

details.gloss: Austrian forces defeated Frederick the Great at Kolin. The battle forced Prussia to abandon the siege of Prague and widened the Seven Years War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Karnal: A painting in Chehel Sotoun2

details.gloss: Nader Shah defeated the Mughal army at Karnal with a smaller force. The victory led to the sack of Delhi and exposed Mughal military decline.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Seringapatam: Tipu death

combatants: British East India Company and allies
details.gloss: British-led forces stormed Seringapatam and killed Tipu Sultan. The siege ended Mysore's strongest resistance to British expansion in southern India.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Long Island: BattleofLongisland

details.gloss: British forces outflanked Washington's army on Long Island and drove it back to Brooklyn Heights. The American escape preserved the Continental Army.
details.casualties: Great Britain: 388 casualties; United States: about 2,000-2,200 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Princeton: The Death of General Mercer at the Battle of Princeton January 3 1777

details.gloss: Washington defeated British troops at Princeton after the surprise at Trenton. The victory revived American morale during a dangerous winter.
details.casualties: United States: about 40 casualties; Great Britain: about 275 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Monmouth: BattleofMonmouth

details.gloss: The Continental Army fought the British to a tactical draw at Monmouth. The battle showed the improved discipline of Washington's army after Valley Forge.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Camden: Battle of Camden

details.gloss: British forces under Cornwallis routed an American army at Camden. The defeat deepened the Patriot crisis in the South and led to new American command under Nathanael Greene.
details.casualties: Great Britain: 324 casualties; United States: about 1,900 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kings Mountain: KingsMountain DeathOfFerguson Chappel
image.attribution: Chappel, Alonzo, 1828-1887 (artist), Jeens, Charles Henry, 1827-1879 (engraver), Virtue & Yorston (publisher)

details.gloss: Patriot militia defeated Loyalists at Kings Mountain during the southern campaign. The victory disrupted British plans to mobilize Loyalist support.
details.casualties: Patriot militia: 88 casualties; Loyalist militia: about 1,100 killed, wounded, or captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Eutaw Springs: Battle of Eutaw Springs (NYPL Hades 254180 478749)

details.outcome: British tactical victory; American strategic gain
details.gloss: Greene's army fought British forces at Eutaw Springs in one of the last major southern battles. The battle further weakened British control inland.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Rivoli: Napoleon at the Battle of Rivoli

details.gloss: Napoleon defeated an Austrian relief attempt at Rivoli. The victory secured French control in northern Italy and confirmed his operational brilliance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Nile: The Battle of the Nile

details.war: French campaign in Egypt and Syria
details.gloss: Nelson destroyed the French fleet at Aboukir Bay. The victory trapped Napoleon's army in Egypt and strengthened British naval dominance.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hohenlinden: Bataille de Hohenlinden

details.gloss: Moreau's French army defeated Austrian and Bavarian forces at Hohenlinden. The victory helped force Austria toward peace and strengthened France's position under the Consulate.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ulm: Charles Thévenin Reddition de la ville d'Ulm

details.gloss: Napoleon encircled Mack's Austrian army around Ulm and forced its surrender. The operation showed the strategic power of rapid corps maneuver before Austerlitz.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Berezina: National Museum in Poznan Przejście przez Berezynę

details.gloss: Napoleon's retreating army forced a crossing of the Berezina under pressure from Russian forces. The battle saved part of the army while underscoring the catastrophe of the Russian campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bautzen: Battle of Bautzen 1813 by Bellange

details.gloss: Napoleon defeated Russo-Prussian forces at Bautzen but failed to destroy them. The incomplete victory contributed to an armistice that let the coalition recover.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dresden: Napoleon.Dresden
image.attribution: Carle Vernet (1758 - 1836) and Jacques François Swebach (1769-1823) JoJan - artwork by Vernet and Swebach

details.gloss: Napoleon defeated a large coalition army at Dresden. It was one of his last major victories before the strategic disaster at Leipzig.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Magenta: Napoléon III et l'Italie Gerolamo Induno La bataille de Magenta 001

details.gloss: French and Sardinian forces defeated Austria at Magenta. The victory helped open Lombardy during the campaign for Italian unification.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Second Battle of Bull Run: Second Battle of Bull Run

details.gloss: Robert E. Lee and Stonewall Jackson defeated John Pope's Union army at Second Bull Run. The victory opened the way for Lee's first invasion of the North.
details.casualties: Confederate States: 7,298 casualties; United States: 14,462 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Chattanooga: Battle of Chattanooga Thulstrup

details.gloss: Union forces broke Confederate positions around Chattanooga after a series of attacks including Missionary Ridge. The victory opened the route into Georgia.
details.casualties: United States: 5,824 casualties; Confederate States: 6,667 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mobile Bay: Bataille de la baie de Mobile par Louis Prang (1824 1909)

details.gloss: Farragut's fleet forced its way into Mobile Bay and defeated Confederate naval defenses. The victory closed one of the Confederacy's last major Gulf ports.
details.casualties: United States Navy: about 320 casualties; Confederate States: about 1,500 killed, wounded, or captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mars-la-Tour: Battle of Mars La Tour, August 16,1870 by Emil Hünten

details.gloss: Prussian forces blocked the French Army of the Rhine from retreating west at Mars-la-Tour. The battle helped trap Bazaine's army around Metz.
details.casualties: Prussia: about 16,000 casualties; France: about 17,000 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Gravelotte: AlphonseNeuvilleFriedhofSaint PrivatL1100806 (2)

details.gloss: German armies fought the French at Gravelotte in the largest battle of the Franco-Prussian War. The result pinned the French army inside Metz.
details.casualties: Prussia and German allies: about 20,000 casualties; France: about 12,000 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Worth: Wuerttemberger bei Woerth

details.gloss: German forces defeated MacMahon's army at Worth early in the Franco-Prussian War. The victory opened Alsace and helped set up the campaign toward Sedan.
details.casualties: Prussia and German allies: about 10,600 casualties; France: about 19,000 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Paardeberg: Canadian Infantry at Paardeberg, 1900

details.gloss: British forces surrounded and forced the surrender of Piet Cronje's Boer army at Paardeberg. The victory shifted momentum in the Second Boer War.
details.casualties: British Empire: about 1,270 casualties; Boer republics: about 4,100 killed, wounded, or captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Colenso: Boers at Colenso

details.gloss: Boer defenders defeated a British attempt to relieve Ladysmith at Colenso. The battle was part of Britain's humiliating Black Week.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Magersfontein: British in Barbed Wire Magersfontein

details.gloss: Boer forces entrenched at the foot of hills surprised and defeated a British attack at Magersfontein. The battle deepened the crisis of Black Week.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tel el-Kebir: Tel El Kebir1882

details.gloss: British forces launched a dawn assault on Egyptian entrenchments at Tel el-Kebir. The victory secured British control over Egypt and the Suez route.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Maiwand: Royal Horse Artillery fleeing from Afghan attack at the Battle of Maiwand

details.gloss: Ayub Khan's Afghan army defeated a British-Indian brigade near Maiwand. The defeat became one of the most famous British setbacks in Afghanistan.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Delhi: 1857 cashmeri gate delhi

details.gloss: Company forces besieged and recaptured Delhi from rebel troops during the Indian Rebellion of 1857. The fall of the city was a central turning point in the uprising.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Coronel: Die Seeschlacht bei Coronel. Gemälde von Hans Bohrdt

details.gloss: German cruisers under Spee destroyed a British squadron off Chile. Coronel was Britain's first major naval defeat in more than a century.
details.casualties: German East Asia Squadron: 3 wounded; Royal Navy: 1,654 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Falkland Islands: Battle of the Falkland Islands, 1914 (retouched)

details.gloss: British battlecruisers destroyed Spee's squadron near the Falklands. The battle avenged Coronel and removed a major German raiding threat.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dogger Bank: HMS Lion was the flagship of Rear Admiral David Beatty during the Battle of Dogger Bank on 24 January 1915. Art.IWMART5205

details.gloss: British battlecruisers intercepted German ships at Dogger Bank. The action exposed command and signaling problems on both sides before Jutland.
details.casualties: Royal Navy: 95 casualties; Imperial German Navy: about 950 casualties

title: First Battle of the Masurian Lakes

image.alt: Historical image associated with First Battle of the Masurian Lakes: EasternFront1914a
image.attribution: Department of Military Art and Engineering, at the U.S. Military Academy (West Point)

details.gloss: German forces drove the Russian First Army out of East Prussia after Tannenberg. The battle stabilized Germany's northeastern frontier in 1914.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of St Quentin Canal: Breaking the hindenburg line

details.gloss: Allied troops broke through the Hindenburg Line at the St Quentin Canal. The victory accelerated Germany's collapse on the Western Front.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Dobro Pole: Ground Cover of the 21st infantry regiment on the Dobro Polje

details.gloss: Allied forces broke through Bulgarian positions at Dobro Pole. The defeat pushed Bulgaria toward armistice and opened the Central Powers' Balkan flank.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cer: Battle of Cer

details.gloss: Serbian forces defeated Austria-Hungary at Cer in the first months of World War I. It was one of the first Allied victories of the war.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kolubara: Battle of Kolubara

details.gloss: Serbia counterattacked and drove Austro-Hungarian forces out after the Battle of Kolubara. The victory delayed Central Powers plans in the Balkans.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Przemysl: Природа и люди 1915 Взятый русской армией Перемышль
image.attribution: не известен, в первоисточнике не указан, unk

details.gloss: Russian forces captured the Austro-Hungarian fortress of Przemysl after a long siege. It was one of the largest fortress surrenders of World War I.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Sidi Barrani: Italian prisoners captured at Sidi Barrani are marched into captivity
image.attribution: No 1 Army Film & Photographic Unit, Keating G (Capt)

details.gloss: British Commonwealth forces attacked Italian camps around Sidi Barrani and began Operation Compass. The victory shattered Italy's forward position in Egypt.
details.casualties: British Commonwealth forces: about 624 casualties; Italy: about 38,000 captured plus killed and wounded

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Beda Fomm: The British Army in North Africa 1940 E443.2

details.gloss: British forces cut off and captured much of Italy's retreating Tenth Army at Beda Fomm. The victory capped the stunning early success of Operation Compass.
details.casualties: British Commonwealth forces: about 500 casualties; Italy: about 25,000 captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Odessa: Roemeense soldaten marcheren de zojuist door de Duitsers en Roemenen veroverde havenstad Odessa in Oekraïne binnen 108005
image.attribution: Fotograaf Onbekend / Fotobureau onbekend / Spaarnestad Photo (05887)

details.gloss: Romanian and German forces besieged Soviet-held Odessa for more than two months. The defense tied down Axis forces before the city was evacuated.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Siege of Sevastopol: Bundesarchiv N 1603 Bild 121, Russland, Sewastopol, zerstörter Hafen

details.gloss: German and Romanian forces captured Sevastopol after a long siege and massive artillery bombardment. The battle secured Crimea for the Axis for a time.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Second Battle of Kharkov: Italians repairing a road during the battle

details.gloss: A Soviet offensive near Kharkov was encircled and destroyed by German forces. The defeat weakened Soviet armies before Germany's 1942 summer offensive.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Korsun-Cherkassy Pocket: RIAN archive 606710 Tank assault force in Korsun Shevchenkovski region

details.gloss: Soviet forces encircled German troops in the Korsun-Cherkassy pocket. Many Germans broke out, but the battle continued the Wehrmacht's retreat in Ukraine.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Tinian: A Water Buffalo, loaded with Marines, churns through the sea bound for beaches of Tinian Island near Guam. NARA 513181
image.attribution: Unknown author Unknown author or not provided

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US forces captured Tinian after an efficient amphibious landing in the Marianas. The island became a major B-29 base, including for the atomic bombing missions against Japan.
details.casualties: United States: 328 killed and 1,571 wounded; Empire of Japan: about 8,000 killed and 300 captured

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Makin: 165th Infantry assault wave attacking Butaritari NARA 531172 restored
image.attribution: Unknown author Unknown author or not provided

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US forces captured Makin during the Gilbert Islands campaign. The operation accompanied Tarawa and helped refine American amphibious methods in the central Pacific.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Eastern Solomons: USS Enterprise (CV 6) under attack and burning during the Battle of the Eastern Solomons on 24 August 1942 (NH 97778)

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: American and Japanese carrier forces clashed during the Guadalcanal campaign. The battle helped prevent Japanese reinforcement of the island.
details.casualties: Allied naval forces: about 90 killed; Imperial Japanese Navy: about 290 killed

title: Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands: USS Enterprise (CV 6) under attack by dive bombers during the Battle of Santa Cruz Islands on 26 October 1942 (80 G 20989)
image.attribution: Official U.S. Navy photo 80-G-20989 from the U.S. Naval History and Heritage Command

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Japanese carriers inflicted heavy losses on the US Navy near the Santa Cruz Islands. The battle cost Japan's experienced aircrews it could not replace and helped decide the Guadalcanal struggle.
details.casualties: Imperial Japanese Navy: about 400-500 killed; United States Navy: 266 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Naval Battle of Guadalcanal: Naval Battle of Guadalcanal

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: US and Japanese warships fought a brutal series of night actions off Guadalcanal. The American victory helped end Japan's effort to retake Henderson Field.
details.casualties: United States Navy: about 1,700 killed; Imperial Japanese Navy: about 1,900 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Cape Esperance: AobaEsperance

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: American cruisers and destroyers intercepted Japanese ships near Cape Esperance. The action was an early US surface victory in the Guadalcanal campaign.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Luzon: Baleta Pass, near Baugio, Luzon

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

details.gloss: Allied forces landed on Luzon and fought to liberate the Philippines' main island. The campaign included Manila and prolonged fighting in the mountains.
details.casualties: 332,330-345,330, including sick

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Rimini: Bundesarchiv Bild 101I 315 1117 18, Italien, LKW auf überfluteter Landstraße

details.gloss: Allied forces broke through toward Rimini during attacks on the Gothic Line. The battle was one of the heaviest fought in Italy in 1944 and helped open the Adriatic flank.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Pusan Perimeter: 27th Infantry Regiment soldiers at the Pusan Perimeter, September 4, 1950

details.gloss: UN and South Korean forces held a shrinking perimeter around Pusan against North Korean attacks. The defense preserved a foothold for the Inchon counteroffensive.
details.casualties: United Nations Command: about 60,000 casualties; North Korea: about 58,000 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Kapyong: Kapyong South Korea 1952 (AWM HOBJ3147)

details.gloss: Australian, Canadian, New Zealand, and other UN forces stopped Chinese attacks near Kapyong. The defense helped protect Seoul during the spring offensive.
details.casualties: United Nations Command: about 100 casualties; China: about 1,000 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Bloody Ridge: Bloody Ridge Memorial 2009

details.gloss: UN forces captured a heavily contested ridge after costly fighting with North Korean troops. The battle reflected the Korean War's shift toward attritional hill combat.
details.casualties: United Nations Command: about 2,700 casualties; North Korea: about 15,000 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Heartbreak Ridge: KOREA.KOREAN CONFLICT Heartbreak Ridge in Korea as seen from the north. NARA FILE 111 SC 382926

details.gloss: UN forces fought for weeks to capture Heartbreak Ridge. The battle became a symbol of the costly stalemate phase of the Korean War.
details.casualties: United Nations Command: about 3,700 casualties; North Korea and China: about 25,000 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ap Bac: Chopper wreck at Ap Bac LF

combatants: South Vietnam and United States advisers
details.gloss: Viet Cong forces resisted a South Vietnamese operation supported by American advisers and helicopters. Ap Bac revealed serious weaknesses in South Vietnam's army.
details.casualties: Viet Cong: about 57 casualties; South Vietnam and United States advisers: about 220 casualties

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Hamburger Hill: Soldiers of the 101st Airborne Division climb Hamburger Hill after the battle in May 1969
image.attribution: United States Army Military History Institute (USAMHI)

details.outcome: American and South Vietnamese tactical victory
details.gloss: US and South Vietnamese troops captured Dong Ap Bia after repeated assaults. The battle became controversial because the hill was abandoned soon afterward.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Valley of Tears: Destroyed Syrian T 62 with Fallen Crew Member Somewhere in the Golan Sector, Yom Kippur War, 8 October 1973
image.attribution: Israel Press and Photo Agency (I.P.P.A.) photographer

details.gloss: Israeli armored units held against Syrian attacks in the Valley of Tears. The battle helped stop Syria's breakthrough on the Golan Heights.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Ammunition Hill: Tahmoshet

details.gloss: Israeli paratroopers captured Ammunition Hill from Jordanian defenders during the fight for Jerusalem. The battle became one of Israel's best-known actions of the Six-Day War.
details.casualties: Israel: 36 killed; Jordan: about 70 killed

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Mount Gaurus: Pozzuoli NASA ISS004 E 5376 added names

details.gloss: Livy presents Mount Gaurus as the first Roman battle against the Samnites. The traditional Roman victory opened the First Samnite War in Campania.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of the Caudine Forks: Battle of the Caudine Forks

details.gloss: Samnite forces trapped a Roman army at the Caudine Forks and forced its surrender. The humiliation became a defining Roman memory of the Second Samnite War.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Battle of Aqaba: T E Lawrence and the Arab Revolt 1916 1918 Q59193
image.attribution: Thomas Edward Lawrence / Imperial War Museums

details.gloss: Arab Revolt forces captured Aqaba from the landward side with T. E. Lawrence advising the attack. The victory gave the revolt a Red Sea port and threatened Ottoman communications.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Capture of Damascus: Damascus city square 1918

details.gloss: British, Indian, Australian, and Arab forces entered Damascus after the victory at Megiddo. The capture marked the collapse of Ottoman control in Syria.

image.alt: Historical image associated with Sack of Constantinople: Frammenti di mosaico pavimentale del 1213, 09
